%w\", err)\n81\t\t}\n82\t\n83\t\t// Create metadata cache\n84\t\tcache := backend.NewMetadataCache(cfg.CacheMaxEntries, cfg.CacheTTL)\n85\t\n86\t\t// Create footer cache (for Parquet footer pinning)\n87\t\tfooterCache := backend.NewFooterCache(cfg.CacheMaxEntries, cfg.CacheTTL)\n88\t\n89\t\t// Create list cache (nil when TTL is 0, meaning disabled)\n90\t\tvar listCache *backend.ListCache\n91\t\tif cfg.ListCacheTTL > 0 {\n92\t\t\tlistCache = backend.NewListCache(cfg.ListCacheMaxEntries, cfg.ListCacheTTL)\n93\t\t}\n94\t\n95\t\t// Create key manager\n96\t\t// Convert config.KeyRoutes to keymanager.Route format\n97\t\tvar routes []keymanager.Route\n98\t\tfor _, r := range cfg.KeyRoutes {\n99\t\t\troutes = append(routes, keymanager.Route{\n100\t\t\t\tPrefix:  r.Prefix,\n101\t\t\t\tKeyName: r.KeyName,\n102\t\t\t})\n103\t\t}\n104\t\tkeyMgr, err := keymanager.New(cfg.MEK, cfg.NamedKeys, routes)\n105\t\tif err != nil {\n106\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create key manager: %w\", err)\n107\t\t}\n108\t\n109\t\t// Create canary monitor\n110\t\tcanaryMonitor := canary.NewMonitor(canary.Config{\n111\t\t\tBackend:    b2Backend,\n112\t\t\tBucket:     cfg.Bucket,\n113\t\t\tMEK:        cfg.MEK,\n114\t\t\tBlockSize:  cfg.BlockSize,\n115\t\t\tInstanceID: cfg.WriterID,\n116\t\t\tInterval:   5 * time.Minute,\n117\t\t\tCanarySize: 1024,\n118\t\t\tMaxRetries: 3,\n119\t\t\tRetryDelay: 10 * time.Second,\n120\t\t})\n121\t\n122\t\t// Create provenance manager\n123\t\tprovenanceMgr := provenance.NewManager(b2Backend, cfg.Bucket, cfg.WriterID)\n124\t\n125\t\t// Create logger\n126\t\tlogger := logging.New(\"armor\")\n127\t\n128\t\t// Create presign signer\n129\t\tpresigner := presign.NewSigner(cfg.PresignSecret, cfg.PresignBaseURL)\n130\t\n131\t\t// Create B2 keys client (for B2 native API key management)\n132\t\t// Uses the same B2 credentials as the S3 backend\n133\t\tvar b2keysClient *b2keys.Client\n134\t\tif cfg.Bucket != \"\" {\n135\t\t\t// Bucket-scoped configuration: application key is restricted to a single bucket\n136\t\t\t// and cannot call account-level B2 API endpoints (like b2_authorize_account).\n137\t\t\t// Key management is unavailable in this mode, which is expected and intentional.\n138\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"B2 key management disabled (application key is bucket-scoped)\")\n139\t\t} else {\n140\t\t\t// No bucket restriction: attempt to create B2 keys client for account-level operations\n141\t\t\t// Extract account ID from the key ID (B2 key IDs are in format accountIdKeyId)\n142\t\t\t// The account ID is the first 12 characters\n143\t\t\taccountID := cfg.B2AccessKeyID\n144\t\t\tif len(accountID) > 12 {\n145\t\t\t\taccountID = accountID[:12]\n146\t\t\t}\n147\t\t\tb2keysClient, err = b2keys.NewClient(context.Background(), accountID, cfg.B2SecretAccessKey)\n148\t\t\tif err != nil {\n149\t\t\t\tlogger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n150\t\t\t\t\t\"error\": err.Error(),\n151\t\t\t\t}).Warn(\"Failed to create B2 keys client - key management disabled\")\n152\t\t\t}\n153\t\t}\n154\t\n155\t\t// Create dashboard with optional authentication\n156\t\tdash := dashboard.NewWithAuth(b2Backend, cfg.Bucket, metrics.DefaultMetrics,\n157\t\t\tcfg.DashboardUser, cfg.DashboardPass, cfg.DashboardToken)\n158\t\n159\t\t// Load manifest index from B2 (startup load).\n160\t\t// The manifest is a performance optimisation — errors are logged as warnings\n161\t\t// and do not prevent startup.\n162\t\tvar manifestIdx *manifest.Index\n163\t\tif cfg.ManifestEnabled {\n164\t\t\tmanifestIdx = manifest.New()\n165\t\t\t// ListRaw bypasses the .armor/ filter so manifest keys are discoverable.\n166\t\t\tlister := func(ctx context.Context, prefix, token string) ([]string, string, error) {\n167\t\t\t\tresult, lerr := b2Backend.ListRaw(ctx, cfg.Bucket, prefix, \"\", token, 1000)\n168\t\t\t\tif lerr != nil {\n169\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, \"\", lerr\n170\t\t\t\t}\n171\t\t\t\tkeys := make([]string, len(result.Objects))\n172\t\t\t\tfor i, obj := range result.Objects {\n173\t\t\t\t\tkeys[i] = obj.Key\n174\t\t\t\t}\n175\t\t\t\treturn keys, result.NextToken, nil\n176\t\t\t}\n177\t\t\t// Fetch snapshot and delta blobs via Cloudflare for free egress and edge\n178\t\t\t// caching. Fall back to direct B2 if no Cloudflare domain is configured.\n179\t\t\tfetcher := func(ctx context.Context, key string) ([]byte, error) {\n180\t\t\t\tvar body io.ReadCloser\n181\t\t\t\tvar ferr error\n182\t\t\t\tif cfg.CFDomain != \"\" {\n183\t\t\t\t\tbody, _, ferr = b2Backend.Get(ctx, cfg.Bucket, key)\n184\t\t\t\t} else {\n185\t\t\t\t\tbody, _, ferr = b2Backend.GetDirect(ctx, cfg.Bucket, key)\n186\t\t\t\t}\n187\t\t\t\tif ferr != nil {\n188\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, ferr\n189\t\t\t\t}\n190\t\t\t\tdefer body.Close()\n191\t\t\t\treturn io.ReadAll(body)\n192\t\t\t}\n193\t\t\tloadStart := time.Now()\n194\t\t\tif loadErr := manifest.Load(context.Background(), manifestIdx, cfg.ManifestPrefix, cfg.WriterID, lister, fetcher); loadErr != nil {\n195\t\t\t\tlogger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n196\t\t\t\t\t\"error\": loadErr.Error(),\n197\t\t\t\t}).Warn(\"manifest startup load failed — continuing with empty manifest index\")\n198\t\t\t\tmanifestIdx = manifest.New() // reset to empty on error\n199\t\t\t} else {\n200\t\t\t\tlogger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n201\t\t\t\t\t\"entries\":  manifestIdx.Len(),\n202\t\t\t\t\t\"seq\":      manifestIdx.Seq(),\n203\t\t\t\t\t\"duration\": time.Since(loadStart).String(),\n204\t\t\t\t}).Info(\"manifest index loaded\")\n205\t\t\t}\n206\t\t}\n207\t\n208\t\t// Create and start the manifest writer (async delta persistence to B2).\n209\t\t// Uploads go direct to B2 (free ingress); no Cloudflare path needed here.\n210\t\tvar manifestWriter *manifest.Writer\n211\t\tvar manifestCompactor *manifest.Compactor\n212\t\tif manifestIdx != nil {\n213\t\t\tuploader := func(ctx context.Context, key string, data []byte) error {\n214\t\t\t\treturn b2Backend.Put(ctx, cfg.Bucket, key, bytes.NewReader(data), int64(len(data)), nil)\n215\t\t\t}\n216\t\t\tmanifestWriter = manifest.NewWriter(manifestIdx, cfg.ManifestPrefix, cfg.WriterID, uploader, 0)\n217\t\n218\t\t\t// Create compactor that lists and batch-deletes via the B2 backend.\n219\t\t\tlisterForCompactor := func(ctx context.Context, prefix, token string) ([]string, string, error) {\n220\t\t\t\tresult, lerr := b2Backend.ListRaw(ctx, cfg.Bucket, prefix, \"\", token, 1000)\n221\t\t\t\tif lerr != nil {\n222\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, \"\", lerr\n223\t\t\t\t}\n224\t\t\t\tkeys := make([]string, len(result.Objects))\n225\t\t\t\tfor i, obj := range result.Objects {\n226\t\t\t\t\tkeys[i] = obj.Key\n227\t\t\t\t}\n228\t\t\t\treturn keys, result.NextToken, nil\n229\t\t\t}\n230\t\t\tdeleter := func(ctx context.Context, keys []string) error {\n231\t\t\t\treturn b2Backend.DeleteObjects(ctx, cfg.Bucket, keys)\n232\t\t\t}\n233\t\t\tcompactionInterval := time.Duration(cfg.ManifestCompactionInterval) * time.Second\n234\t\t\tmanifestCompactor = manifest.NewCompactor(\n235\t\t\t\tmanifestIdx,\n236\t\t\t\tcfg.ManifestPrefix,\n237\t\t\t\tcfg.WriterID,\n238\t\t\t\tuploader,\n239\t\t\t\tlisterForCompactor,\n240\t\t\t\tdeleter,\n241\t\t\t\tcompactionInterval,\n242\t\t\t\tcfg.ManifestCompactionThreshold,\n243\t\t\t)\n244\t\n245\t\t\t// Wire writer → compactor: after each delta flush, notify compactor.\n246\t\t\tmanifestWriter.SetOnFlush(manifestCompactor.NotifyDelta)\n247\t\n248\t\t\tmanifestWriter.Start(context.Background())\n249\t\t\tmanifestCompactor.Start(context.Background())\n250\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"manifest writer and compactor started\")\n251\t\t}\n252\t\n253\t\treturn &Server{\n254\t\t\tconfig:         cfg,\n255\t\t\tbackend:        b2Backend,\n256\t\t\tcache:          cache,\n257\t\t\tfooterCache:    footerCache,\n258\t\t\tlistCache:      listCache,\n259\t\t\tkeyManager:     keyMgr,\n260\t\t\tcanary:         canaryMonitor,\n261\t\t\tcanaryDisabled: cfg.CanaryDisabled,\n262\t\t\tprovenance:     provenanceMgr,\n263\t\t\tpresigner:      presigner,\n264\t\t\tb2keys:         b2keysClient,\n265\t\t\tdashboard:      dash,\n266\t\t\tmanifest:          manifestIdx,\n267\t\t\tmanifestWriter:    manifestWriter,\n268\t\t\tmanifestCompactor: manifestCompactor,\n269\t\t\tmetrics:        metrics.DefaultMetrics,\n270\t\t\trequestTracker: metrics.DefaultRequestTracker,\n271\t\t\tlogger:         logger,\n272\t\t}, nil\n273\t}\n274\t\n275\t// manifestRecorder implements handlers.ManifestRecorder. It updates the\n276\t// in-memory index synchronously (so subsequent HeadObject calls see the new\n277\t// state immediately) and enqueues the op for async B2 delta persistence.\n278\ttype manifestRecorder struct {\n279\t\tidx    *manifest.Index\n280\t\twriter *manifest.Writer\n281\t}\n282\t\n283\tfunc (m *manifestRecorder) RecordPut(bucket, key string, size int64, sha256Hex string, iv, wrappedDEK []byte, blockSize int, contentType, etag string) {\n284\t\tentry := &manifest.Entry{\n285\t\t\tPlaintextSize:   size,\n286\t\t\tPlaintextSHA256: sha256Hex,\n287\t\t\tIV:              iv,\n288\t\t\tWrappedDEK:      wrappedDEK,\n289\t\t\tBlockSize:       blockSize,\n290\t\t\tContentType:     contentType,\n291\t\t\tETag:            etag,\n292\t\t\tLastModified:    time.Now().UTC(),\n293\t\t}\n294\t\tm.idx.Put(bucket, key, entry)\n295\t\tm.writer.EnqueuePut(bucket, key, entry)\n296\t}\n297\t\n298\tfunc (m *manifestRecorder) RecordDelete(bucket, key string) {\n299\t\tm.idx.Delete(bucket, key)\n300\t\tm.writer.EnqueueDelete(bucket, key)\n301\t}\n302\t\n303\tfunc (m *manifestRecorder) Lookup(bucket, key string) (*handlers.ManifestEntry, bool) {\n304\t\tentry, ok := m.idx.Get(bucket, key)\n305\t\tif !ok {\n306\t\t\treturn nil, false\n307\t\t}\n308\t\treturn &handlers.ManifestEntry{\n309\t\t\tPlaintextSize: entry.PlaintextSize,\n310\t\t\tContentType:   entry.ContentType,\n311\t\t\tETag:          entry.ETag,\n312\t\t\tLastModified:  entry.LastModified,\n313\t\t\tIV:            entry.IV,\n314\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    entry.WrappedDEK,\n315\t\t\tBlockSize:     entry.BlockSize,\n316\t\t}, true\n317\t}\n318\t\n319\t// StopManifestWriter flushes any pending manifest ops and stops the async writer.\n320\tfunc (s *Server) StopManifestWriter() {\n321\t\tif s.manifestWriter != nil {\n322\t\t\ts.manifestWriter.Stop()\n323\t\t\ts.logger.Info(\"manifest writer stopped\")\n324\t\t}\n325\t}\n326\t\n327\t// StopManifestCompactor stops the background compaction goroutine.\n328\tfunc (s *Server) StopManifestCompactor() {\n329\t\tif s.manifestCompactor != nil {\n330\t\t\ts.manifestCompactor.Stop()\n331\t\t\ts.logger.Info(\"manifest compactor stopped\")\n332\t\t}\n333\t}\n334\t\n335\t// StartCanary starts the canary monitor.\n336\t// It should be called after the server is created.\n337\t// When ARMOR_CANARY_DISABLED=true, the monitor is not started and /readyz\n338\t// always returns 200 regardless of B2 reachability.\n339\tfunc (s *Server) StartCanary(ctx context.Context) {\n340\t\tif s.canary == nil || s.canaryStarted {\n341\t\t\treturn\n342\t\t}\n343\t\tif s.canaryDisabled {\n344\t\t\ts.logger.Info(\"Canary monitor disabled (ARMOR_CANARY_DISABLED=true)\")\n345\t\t\treturn\n346\t\t}\n347\t\ts.canaryStarted = true\n348\t\ts.canary.Start(ctx)\n349\t\ts.logger.Info(\"Canary monitor started\")\n350\t}\n351\t\n352\t// StopCanary stops the canary monitor.\n353\tfunc (s *Server) StopCanary() {\n354\t\tif s.canary != nil && s.canaryStarted {\n355\t\t\ts.canary.Stop()\n356\t\t\ts.logger.Info(\"Canary monitor stopped\")\n357\t\t}\n358\t}\n359\t\n360\t// Handler returns the main S3 API handler.\n361\tfunc (s *Server) Handler() http.Handler {\n362\t\tmux := http.NewServeMux()\n363\t\n364\t\t// Health endpoints\n365\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/healthz\", s.healthz)\n366\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/readyz\", s.readyz)\n367\t\n368\t\t// Share endpoint for pre-signed URLs (public, no auth required)\n369\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/share/\", s.handleShare)\n370\t\n371\t\t// S3 operations\n372\t\th := handlers.New(s.config, s.backend, s.cache, s.footerCache, s.keyManager, s.listCache)\n373\t\n374\t\t// Wire up provenance if available\n375\t\tif s.provenance != nil {\n376\t\t\th.WithProvenance(s.provenance)\n377\t\t}\n378\t\n379\t\t// Wire up manifest recorder if enabled\n380\t\tif s.manifest != nil && s.manifestWriter != nil {\n381\t\t\th.WithManifest(&manifestRecorder{idx: s.manifest, writer: s.manifestWriter})\n382\t\t}\n383\t\n384\t\t// Bucket operations\n385\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/\", s.wrapHandler(h.HandleRoot))\n386\t\n387\t\treturn mux\n388\t}\n389\t\n390\t// AdminHandler returns the admin API handler.\n391\tfunc (s *Server) AdminHandler() http.Handler {\n392\t\tmux := http.NewServeMux()\n393\t\n394\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/healthz\", s.healthz)\n395\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/key/verify\", s.verifyKey)\n396\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/key/rotate\", s.rotateKey)\n397\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/key/export\", s.exportKey)\n398\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/armor/canary\", s.canaryHandler)\n399\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/armor/audit\", s.audit)\n400\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/presign\", s.handlePresign)\n401\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/b2/keys\", s.handleB2Keys)           // GET=List, POST=Create\n402\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/b2/keys/\", s.handleB2KeyDelete)     // DELETE=Delete by ID\n403\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/metrics\", s.metrics.Handler())\n404\t\n405\t\t// Dashboard routes (authenticated via DashboardUser/Pass or DashboardToken)\n406\t\tif s.dashboard != nil {\n407\t\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard\", s.dashboard.HandlerWithAuth())\n408\t\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/\", s.dashboard.HandlerWithAuth()) // For prefix navigation\n409\t\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/object\", s.dashboard.ObjectDetailHandlerWithAuth())\n410\t\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/metrics\", s.dashboard.MetricsHandlerWithAuth())\n411\t\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/encryption-stats\", s.dashboard.EncryptionStatsHandlerWithAuth())\n412\t\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/api/list\", s.dashboard.ListAPIHandlerWithAuth())\n413\t\n414\t\t\t// Key rotation proxy handler (authenticated)\n415\t\t\tadminClient := &http.Client{\n416\t\t\t\tTimeout: 30 * time.Minute, // Key rotation can take a long time\n417\t\t\t}\n418\t\t\tadminURL := \"http://\" + s.config.AdminListen + \"/admin/key/rotate\"\n419\t\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/admin/key/rotate\", s.dashboard.KeyRotateHandlerWithAuth(adminClient, adminURL))\n420\t\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/admin/key/status\", s.dashboard.KeyRotateStatusHandlerWithAuth())\n421\t\t}\n422\t\n423\t\treturn mux\n424\t}\n425\t\n426\t// healthz returns the health status.\n427\t// This is the liveness probe — it only checks that the process is alive.\n428\t// Backend connectivity is checked by /readyz (the readiness probe).\n429\tfunc (s *Server) healthz(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n430\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n431\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"OK\"))\n432\t}\n433\t\n434\t// readyz returns the readiness status.\n435\t// When the canary monitor is running and not disabled, its in-memory health\n436\t// state is the sole signal — no backend call is made. When the canary is\n437\t// disabled (ARMOR_CANARY_DISABLED=true), /readyz always returns 200 and the\n438\t// liveness probe (/healthz) is the sole health guard. When the canary is not\n439\t// configured, the manifest writer's last flush is used as the health signal.\n440\tfunc (s *Server) readyz(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n441\t\t// When canary is explicitly disabled, skip all backend checks.\n442\t\tif s.canaryDisabled {\n443\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n444\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Ready\"))\n445\t\t\treturn\n446\t\t}\n447\t\n448\t\t// Canary monitor is authoritative when running.\n449\t\tif s.canary != nil && s.canaryStarted {\n450\t\t\tif !s.canary.IsHealthy() {\n451\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n452\t\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Not ready - canary check failed\"))\n453\t\t\t\treturn\n454\t\t\t}\n455\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n456\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Ready\"))\n457\t\t\treturn\n458\t\t}\n459\t\n460\t\t// Fallback: manifest writer's last successful delta flush.\n461\t\t// A flush within the last 60 seconds indicates the service is healthy.\n462\t\tconst flushThreshold = 60 * time.Second\n463\t\tif s.manifestWriter != nil {\n464\t\t\tlastFlush := s.manifestWriter.LastFlush()\n465\t\t\tif !lastFlush.IsZero() && time.Since(lastFlush) < flushThreshold {\n466\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n467\t\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Ready\"))\n468\t\t\t\treturn\n469\t\t\t}\n470\t\t\t// No recent flush — report unhealthy\n471\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n472\t\t\tif lastFlush.IsZero() {\n473\t\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Not ready - manifest writer has never flushed\"))\n474\t\t\t} else {\n475\t\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(w, \"Not ready - manifest writer last flush %v ago (threshold %v)\", time.Since(lastFlush).Round(time.Second), flushThreshold)\n476\t\t\t}\n477\t\t\treturn\n478\t\t}\n479\t\n480\t\t// Neither canary nor manifest writer available — report unhealthy.\n481\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n482\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Not ready - no health signal available\"))\n483\t}\n484\t\n485\t// verifyKey verifies the MEK is correct.\n486\tfunc (s *Server) verifyKey(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n487\t\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n488\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n489\t\t\treturn\n490\t\t}\n491\t\n492\t\tif s.canary == nil {\n493\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n494\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"unknown\",\"error\":\"canary monitor not configured\"}`))\n495\t\t\treturn\n496\t\t}\n497\t\n498\t\tstatus := s.canary.GetStatus()\n499\t\tif status.DecryptVerified && status.HMACVerified {\n500\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n501\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"verified\",\"message\":\"MEK is correct\"}`))\n502\t\t\treturn\n503\t\t}\n504\t\n505\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n506\t\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"unverified\",\"error\":\"canary check failed - MEK may be incorrect\"}`))\n507\t}\n508\t\n509\t// rotateKey rotates the MEK.\n510\tfunc (s *Server) rotateKey(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n511\t\tif r.Method != http.MethodPost {\n512\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n513\t\t\treturn\n514\t\t}\n515\t\n516\t\t// Read the new MEK from the request body\n517\t\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n518\t\tif err != nil {\n519\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read request body: %v\", err), http.StatusBadRequest)\n520\t\t\treturn\n521\t\t}\n522\t\tdefer r.Body.Close()\n523\t\n524\t\t// The new MEK should be a 32-byte hex-encoded string (64 hex chars)\n525\t\t// or raw 32 bytes\n526\t\tvar newMEK []byte\n527\t\tif len(body) == 64 {\n528\t\t\t// Hex-encoded\n529\t\t\tnewMEK, err = hex.DecodeString(string(body))\n530\t\t\tif err != nil {\n531\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid hex-encoded MEK\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n532\t\t\t\treturn\n533\t\t\t}\n534\t\t} else if len(body) == 32 {\n535\t\t\t// Raw bytes\n536\t\t\tnewMEK = body\n537\t\t} else {\n538\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid MEK length: expected 32 bytes or 64 hex chars, got %d\", len(body)), http.StatusBadRequest)\n539\t\t\treturn\n540\t\t}\n541\t\n542\t\t// Create key rotator with the default key. Pass the manifest index so\n543\t\t// rotateObject can skip per-object HeadObject calls when the entry is cached.\n544\t\tdefaultKey := s.keyManager.DefaultKey()\n545\t\trotator := NewKeyRotator(s.backend, s.config.Bucket, defaultKey.MEK, newMEK, s.manifest)\n546\t\n547\t\t// Perform rotation\n548\t\tresult, err := rotator.Rotate(r.Context())\n549\t\tif err != nil {\n550\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n551\t\t\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{\n552\t\t\t\t\"status\":  \"failed\",\n553\t\t\t\t\"error\":   err.Error(),\n554\t\t\t\t\"result\":  result,\n555\t\t\t})\n556\t\t\treturn\n557\t\t}\n558\t\n559\t\t// Update the server's MEK on success\n560\t\tif result.Status == \"completed\" {\n561\t\t\tif err := s.keyManager.UpdateDefaultKey(newMEK); err != nil {\n562\t\t\t\ts.logger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n563\t\t\t\t\t\"error\": err.Error(),\n564\t\t\t\t}).Error(\"failed to update key manager after rotation\")\n565\t\t\t}\n566\t\t\t// Clear the metadata cache since DEKs are now wrapped with new MEK\n567\t\t\ts.cache.Clear()\n568\t\t}\n569\t\n570\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n571\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n572\t\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(result)\n573\t}\n574\t\n575\t// exportKey exports the current MEK.\n576\tfunc (s *Server) exportKey(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n577\t\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n578\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n579\t\t\treturn\n580\t\t}\n581\t\n582\t\tif r.URL.Query().Get(\"confirm\") != \"yes\" {\n583\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Must include ?confirm=yes to export key\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n584\t\t\treturn\n585\t\t}\n586\t\n587\t\t// Export the default MEK as hex-encoded string\n588\t\tdefaultKey := s.keyManager.DefaultKey()\n589\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n590\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n591\t\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{\n592\t\t\t\"mek\":   hex.EncodeToString(defaultKey.MEK),\n593\t\t\t\"format\": \"hex\",\n594\t\t\t\"warning\": \"This key provides access to all encrypted data. Store securely.\",\n595\t\t})\n596\t}\n597\t\n598\t// canaryHandler returns the canary status.\n599\tfunc (s *Server) canaryHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n600\t\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n601\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n602\t\t\treturn\n603\t\t}\n604\t\n605\t\tif s.canary == nil {\n606\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n607\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"unknown\",\"error\":\"canary monitor not configured\"}`))\n608\t\t\treturn\n609\t\t}\n610\t\n611\t\tstatus := s.canary.GetStatus()\n612\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n613\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n614\t\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(status)\n615\t}\n616\t\n617\t// audit returns the audit status.\n618\tfunc (s *Server) audit(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n619\t\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n620\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n621\t\t\treturn\n622\t\t}\n623\t\n624\t\t// Create auditor and perform audit\n625\t\tauditor := provenance.NewAuditor(s.backend, s.config.Bucket)\n626\t\tresult, err := auditor.Audit(r.Context())\n627\t\tif err != nil {\n628\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n629\t\t\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{\n630\t\t\t\t\"status\": \"error\",\n631\t\t\t\t\"error\":   err.Error(),\n632\t\t\t})\n633\t\t\treturn\n634\t\t}\n635\t\n636\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n637\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n638\t\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(result)\n639\t}\n640\t\n641\t// wrapHandler wraps a handler with common middleware.\n642\tfunc (s *Server) wrapHandler(h http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\n643\t\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n644\t\t\tstart := time.Now()\n645\t\n646\t\t\t// Track in-flight request\n647\t\t\ts.requestTracker.Start()\n648\t\t\tdefer s.requestTracker.End()\n649\t\n650\t\t\t// Log request\n651\t\t\ts.logger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n652\t\t\t\t\"method\": r.Method,\n653\t\t\t\t\"path\":   r.URL.Path,\n654\t\t\t}).Debug(\"incoming request\")\n655\t\n656\t\t\t// Add CORS headers for browser clients\n657\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\", \"*\")\n658\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\", \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\")\n659\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\", \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\")\n660\t\n661\t\t\t// Handle preflight\n662\t\t\tif r.Method == http.MethodOptions {\n663\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n664\t\t\t\treturn\n665\t\t\t}\n666\t\n667\t\t\t// Verify auth for non-public endpoints\n668\t\t\tif !s.isPublicPath(r.URL.Path) {\n669\t\t\t\tcred, err := s.verifyAuthAndGetCredential(r)\n670\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n671\t\t\t\t\tif authErr, ok := err.(*AuthError); ok {\n672\t\t\t\t\t\ts.writeError(w, authErr.Code, authErr.Message, 403)\n673\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n674\t\t\t\t\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Invalid credentials\", 403)\n675\t\t\t\t\t}\n676\t\t\t\t\ts.metrics.IncRequestsTotal(\"auth\", 403)\n677\t\t\t\t\treturn\n678\t\t\t\t}\n679\t\t\t\t// Check ACL for the request. For list operations the URL path has no\n680\t\t\t\t// key component, so fall back to the ?prefix query param so that ACL\n681\t\t\t\t// prefix restrictions are enforced correctly against the listed prefix.\n682\t\t\t\tbucket, key := s.extractBucketAndKey(r)\n683\t\t\t\tif key == \"\" {\n684\t\t\t\t\tkey = r.URL.Query().Get(\"prefix\")\n685\t\t\t\t}\n686\t\t\t\tif err := CheckACL(cred, bucket, key); err != nil {\n687\t\t\t\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Access Denied\", 403)\n688\t\t\t\t\ts.metrics.IncRequestsTotal(\"acl\", 403)\n689\t\t\t\t\treturn\n690\t\t\t\t}\n691\t\t\t}\n692\t\n693\t\t\t// Decode aws-chunked body when MinIO streaming signature is used.\n694\t\t\t// The seed signature in the Authorization header already authenticated the\n695\t\t\t// request; we only need to strip the chunk framing to recover the payload.\n696\t\t\tif r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\") == \"STREAMING-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256-PAYLOAD\" {\n697\t\t\t\tif decoded := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Decoded-Content-Length\"); decoded != \"\" {\n698\t\t\t\t\tif n, err := strconv.ParseInt(decoded, 10, 64); err == nil {\n699\t\t\t\t\t\tr.ContentLength = n\n700\t\t\t\t\t}\n701\t\t\t\t}\n702\t\t\t\tr.Body = newAWSChunkedReader(r.Body)\n703\t\t\t}\n704\t\n705\t\t\t// Use a response writer wrapper to capture status code\n706\t\t\trw := &responseWriter{ResponseWriter: w, statusCode: http.StatusOK}\n707\t\n708\t\t\th(rw, r)\n709\t\n710\t\t\t// Record metrics\n711\t\t\tduration := time.Since(start)\n712\t\t\ts.metrics.IncRequestsTotal(r.Method, rw.statusCode)\n713\t\t\ts.metrics.RecordRequestDuration(r.Method, duration)\n714\t\n715\t\t\t// Log completed request\n716\t\t\tfields := map[string]interface{}{\n717\t\t\t\t\"method\":      r.Method,\n718\t\t\t\t\"path\":        r.URL.Path,\n719\t\t\t\t\"status\":      rw.statusCode,\n720\t\t\t\t\"duration_ms\": duration.Milliseconds(),\n721\t\t\t}\n722\t\t\tif rng := r.Header.Get(\"Range\"); rng != \"\" {\n723\t\t\t\tfields[\"range\"] = rng\n724\t\t\t}\n725\t\t\ts.logger.WithFields(fields).Info(\"request completed\")\n726\t\t}\n727\t}\n728\t\n729\t// responseWriter wraps http.ResponseWriter to capture the status code.\n730\ttype responseWriter struct {\n731\t\thttp.ResponseWriter\n732\t\tstatusCode int\n733\t}\n734\t\n735\t// WriteHeader captures the status code.\n736\tfunc (rw *responseWriter) WriteHeader(code int) {\n737\t\trw.statusCode = code\n738\t\trw.ResponseWriter.WriteHeader(code)\n739\t}\n740\t\n741\t// isPublicPath checks if a path is public (no auth required).\n742\tfunc (s *Server) isPublicPath(path string) bool {\n743\t\treturn path == \"/healthz\" || path == \"/readyz\"\n744\t}\n745\t\n746\t// verifyAuthAndGetCredential validates AWS SigV4 authentication and returns the credential.\n747\tfunc (s *Server) verifyAuthAndGetCredential(r *http.Request) (*config.Credential, error) {\n748\t\t// Create auth with all credentials\n749\t\tauth := NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials(s.config.Credentials, s.config.B2Region)\n750\t\n751\t\t// Check for query-based auth (presigned URLs)\n752\t\tif r.URL.Query().Get(\"X-Amz-Credential\") != \"\" {\n753\t\t\treturn auth.VerifyQueryAuth(r)\n754\t\t}\n755\t\n756\t\t// Check for header-based auth\n757\t\tauthHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n758\t\tif authHeader == \"\" {\n759\t\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n760\t\t}\n761\t\n762\t\t// Pass nil body — buildCanonicalRequest uses x-amz-content-sha256 header\n763\t\t// when present (which covers all AWS SDK/CLI requests), falling back to\n764\t\t// sha256(body) for requests without that header.\n765\t\treturn auth.VerifyRequest(r, nil)\n766\t}\n767\t\n768\t// extractBucketAndKey extracts bucket and key from the request URL.\n769\tfunc (s *Server) extractBucketAndKey(r *http.Request) (bucket, key string) {\n770\t\tpath := r.URL.Path\n771\t\t// Remove leading slash\n772\t\tpath = strings.TrimPrefix(path, \"/\")\n773\t\n774\t\t// Check for virtual-hosted-style (bucket in host)\n775\t\t// For path-style: /bucket/key\n776\t\tparts := strings.SplitN(path, \"/\", 2)\n777\t\tif len(parts) >= 1 {\n778\t\t\tbucket = parts[0]\n779\t\t}\n780\t\tif len(parts) >= 2 {\n781\t\t\tkey = parts[1]\n782\t\t\t// URL decode the key (DuckDB httpfs encodes special chars like = as %3D)\n783\t\t\tif decoded, err := url.PathUnescape(key); err == nil {\n784\t\t\t\tkey = decoded\n785\t\t\t}\n786\t\t}\n787\t\n788\t\t// Use configured bucket if empty\n789\t\tif bucket == \"\" {\n790\t\t\tbucket = s.config.Bucket\n791\t\t}\n792\t\n793\t\treturn bucket, key\n794\t}\n795\t\n796\t// writeError writes an S3 error response.\n797\tfunc (s *Server) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n798\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n799\t\tw.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n800\t\tvar codeBuf, msgBuf bytes.Buffer\n801\t\txml.EscapeText(&codeBuf, []byte(code))\n802\t\txml.EscapeText(&msgBuf, []byte(message))\n803\t\tfmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`+\"\\n<Error>\\n  <Code>%s</Code>\\n  <Message>%s</Message>\\n</Error>\",\n804\t\t\tcodeBuf.String(), msgBuf.String())\n805\t}\n806\t\n807\t// GenerateDEK generates a new DEK (exposed for handlers).\n808\tfunc (s *Server) GenerateDEK() ([]byte, error) {\n809\t\treturn crypto.GenerateDEK()\n810\t}\n811\t\n812\t// GenerateIV generates a new IV (exposed for handlers).\n813\tfunc (s *Server) GenerateIV() ([]byte, error) {\n814\t\treturn crypto.GenerateIV()\n815\t}\n816\t\n817\t// WaitForInFlightRequests waits for all in-flight requests to complete.\n818\tfunc (s *Server) WaitForInFlightRequests() {\n819\t\ts.requestTracker.Wait()\n820\t}\n821\t\n822\t// InFlightRequestCount returns the current number of in-flight requests.\n823\tfunc (s *Server) InFlightRequestCount() int64 {\n824\t\treturn s.requestTracker.Count()\n825\t}\n826\t\n827\t// handlePresign generates a pre-signed URL for sharing encrypted files.\n828\t// POST /admin/presign\n829\t// Body: {\"bucket\": \"my-bucket\", \"key\": \"path/to/file.parquet\", \"expires_in\": \"1h\", \"content_disposition\": \"attachment; filename=\\\"file.parquet\\\"\"}\n830\tfunc (s *Server) handlePresign(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n831\t\tif r.Method != http.MethodPost {\n832\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n833\t\t\treturn\n834\t\t}\n835\t\n836\t\t// Verify auth\n837\t\tcred, err := s.verifyAuthAndGetCredential(r)\n838\t\tif err != nil {\n839\t\t\t// Return specific authentication error code and message\n840\t\t\tif authErr, ok := err.(*AuthError); ok {\n841\t\t\t\ts.writeError(w, authErr.Code, authErr.Message, 403)\n842\t\t\t} else {\n843\t\t\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Invalid credentials\", 403)\n844\t\t\t}\n845\t\t\treturn\n846\t\t}\n847\t\n848\t\t// Parse request body\n849\t\tvar req struct {\n850\t\t\tBucket             string `json:\"bucket\"`\n851\t\t\tKey                string `json:\"key\"`\n852\t\t\tExpiresIn          string `json:\"expires_in\"`\n853\t\t\tContentDisposition string `json:\"content_disposition\"`\n854\t\t\tRange              string `json:\"range\"`\n855\t\t}\n856\t\n857\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n858\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid request body: %v\", err), http.StatusBadRequest)\n859\t\t\treturn\n860\t\t}\n861\t\n862\t\t// Use configured bucket if not specified\n863\t\tbucket := req.Bucket\n864\t\tif bucket == \"\" {\n865\t\t\tbucket = s.config.Bucket\n866\t\t}\n867\t\n868\t\t// Validate required fields\n869\t\tif req.Key == \"\" {\n870\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"key is required\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n871\t\t\treturn\n872\t\t}\n873\t\n874\t\t// Check ACL for the request\n875\t\tif err := CheckACL(cred, bucket, req.Key); err != nil {\n876\t\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Access Denied\", 403)\n877\t\t\treturn\n878\t\t}\n879\t\n880\t\t// Parse expiration (default 1 hour)\n881\t\texpiration := presign.DefaultExpiration\n882\t\tif req.ExpiresIn != \"\" {\n883\t\t\texpiration, err = presign.ParseExpiration(req.ExpiresIn)\n884\t\t\tif err != nil {\n885\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid expires_in: %v\", err), http.StatusBadRequest)\n886\t\t\t\treturn\n887\t\t\t}\n888\t\t}\n889\t\n890\t\t// Build options\n891\t\tvar opts []presign.Option\n892\t\tif req.ContentDisposition != \"\" {\n893\t\t\topts = append(opts, presign.WithContentDisposition(req.ContentDisposition))\n894\t\t}\n895\t\tif req.Range != \"\" {\n896\t\t\topts = append(opts, presign.WithRange(req.Range))\n897\t\t}\n898\t\n899\t\t// Generate pre-signed URL\n900\t\tshareURL, err := s.presigner.GenerateURL(bucket, req.Key, expiration, opts...)\n901\t\tif err != nil {\n902\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate URL: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n903\t\t\treturn\n904\t\t}\n905\t\n906\t\t// Return response\n907\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n908\t\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{\n909\t\t\t\"url\":       shareURL,\n910\t\t\t\"expires_in\": presign.FormatExpiration(expiration),\n911\t\t\t\"expires_at\": time.Now().Add(expiration).UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),\n912\t\t})\n913\t}\n914\t\n915\t// handleShare serves decrypted content from a pre-signed URL token.\n916\t// GET /share/<token>\n917\tfunc (s *Server) handleShare(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n918\t\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n919\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n920\t\t\treturn\n921\t\t}\n922\t\n923\t\t// Extract token from path\n924\t\ttokenStr := presign.ParseTokenFromPath(r.URL.Path)\n925\t\tif tokenStr == \"\" {\n926\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Missing token\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n927\t\t\treturn\n928\t\t}\n929\t\n930\t\t// Verify token\n931\t\ttoken, err := s.presigner.VerifyToken(tokenStr)\n932\t\tif err != nil {\n933\t\t\tif errors.Is(err, presign.ErrExpiredToken) {\n934\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Link expired\", http.StatusGone)\n935\t\t\t\treturn\n936\t\t\t}\n937\t\t\tif errors.Is(err, presign.ErrInvalidSignature) {\n938\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid link\", http.StatusForbidden)\n939\t\t\t\treturn\n940\t\t\t}\n941\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid token\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n942\t\t\treturn\n943\t\t}\n944\t\n945\t\t// Get object metadata\n946\t\tctx := r.Context()\n947\t\tinfo, err := s.backend.Head(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key)\n948\t\tif err != nil {\n949\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Object not found: %v\", err), http.StatusNotFound)\n950\t\t\treturn\n951\t\t}\n952\t\n953\t\t// Check if object is ARMOR-encrypted\n954\t\tif !info.IsARMOREncrypted {\n955\t\t\t// Serve non-ARMOR objects directly (passthrough)\n956\t\t\tbody, _, err := s.backend.Get(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key)\n957\t\t\tif err != nil {\n958\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n959\t\t\t\treturn\n960\t\t\t}\n961\t\t\tdefer body.Close()\n962\t\n963\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", info.Size))\n964\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", info.ContentType)\n965\t\t\tif token.ContentDisposition != \"\" {\n966\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Disposition\", token.ContentDisposition)\n967\t\t\t}\n968\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n969\t\t\tio.Copy(w, body)\n970\t\t\treturn\n971\t\t}\n972\t\n973\t\t// Parse ARMOR metadata\n974\t\tarmorMeta, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata)\n975\t\tif !ok {\n976\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to parse object metadata\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n977\t\t\treturn\n978\t\t}\n979\t\n980\t\t// Get the MEK for this object\n981\t\tmek, err := s.keyManager.GetMEKByID(armorMeta.KeyID)\n982\t\tif err != nil {\n983\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to get decryption key\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n984\t\t\treturn\n985\t\t}\n986\t\n987\t\t// Unwrap DEK\n988\t\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(mek, armorMeta.WrappedDEK)\n989\t\tif err != nil {\n990\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to unwrap DEK\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n991\t\t\treturn\n992\t\t}\n993\t\n994\t\t// Create decryptor\n995\t\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(dek, armorMeta.IV, armorMeta.BlockSize)\n996\t\tif err != nil {\n997\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to create decryptor\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n998\t\t\treturn\n999\t\t}\n1000\t\n1001\t\t// Set response headers\n1002\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", armorMeta.PlaintextSize))\n1003\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n1004\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n1005\t\tif token.ContentDisposition != \"\" {\n1006\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Disposition\", token.ContentDisposition)\n1007\t\t}\n1008\t\n1009\t\t// Handle range request if specified in token or header\n1010\t\trangeHeader := token.Range\n1011\t\tif rangeHeader == \"\" {\n1012\t\t\trangeHeader = r.Header.Get(\"Range\")\n1013\t\t}\n1014\t\n1015\t\tif rangeHeader != \"\" {\n1016\t\t\ts.handleShareRangeRequest(w, r, token, decryptor, armorMeta, rangeHeader)\n1017\t\t\treturn\n1018\t\t}\n1019\t\n1020\t\t// Full object download\n1021\t\ts.handleShareFullObject(w, r, token, decryptor, armorMeta)\n1022\t}\n1023\t\n1024\t// handleShareFullObject handles full object downloads for share endpoint.\n1025\tfunc (s *Server) handleShareFullObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, token *presign.Token, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata) {\n1026\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1027\t\n1028\t\tblockSize := armorMeta.BlockSize\n1029\t\tblockCount := int(crypto.ComputeBlockCount(armorMeta.PlaintextSize, blockSize))\n1030\t\tplaintextSize := armorMeta.PlaintextSize\n1031\t\n1032\t\t// Calculate offsets\n1033\t\thmacTableOffset := crypto.HeaderSize + plaintextSize\n1034\t\thmacTableSize := int64(blockCount) * crypto.HMACSize\n1035\t\n1036\t\t// 1. Prefetch HMAC table\n1037\t\thmacBody, err := s.backend.GetRange(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key, hmacTableOffset, hmacTableSize)\n1038\t\tif err != nil {\n1039\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to fetch HMAC table: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1040\t\t\treturn\n1041\t\t}\n1042\t\thmacTable, err := io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\n1043\t\thmacBody.Close()\n1044\t\tif err != nil {\n1045\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read HMAC table: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1046\t\t\treturn\n1047\t\t}\n1048\t\n1049\t\t// 2. Stream data from Cloudflare\n1050\t\tstreamSize := crypto.HeaderSize + plaintextSize\n1051\t\tdataBody, err := s.backend.GetRange(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key, 0, streamSize)\n1052\t\tif err != nil {\n1053\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object stream: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1054\t\t\treturn\n1055\t\t}\n1056\t\tdefer dataBody.Close()\n1057\t\n1058\t\t// 3. Read and discard header\n1059\t\theaderBuf := make([]byte, crypto.HeaderSize)\n1060\t\tif _, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, headerBuf); err != nil {\n1061\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read header: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1062\t\t\treturn\n1063\t\t}\n1064\t\n1065\t\t// 4. Write status before streaming\n1066\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1067\t\n1068\t\t// 5. Stream decrypt\n1069\t\tencryptedBuf := make([]byte, blockSize)\n1070\t\tfor blockIndex := 0; blockIndex < blockCount; blockIndex++ {\n1071\t\t\tremaining := plaintextSize - int64(blockIndex)*int64(blockSize)\n1072\t\t\tactualBlockSize := int(min64(int64(blockSize), remaining))\n1073\t\n1074\t\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:actualBlockSize]\n1075\t\t\tn, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, encryptedBuf)\n1076\t\t\tif err != nil && err != io.EOF && err != io.ErrUnexpectedEOF {\n1077\t\t\t\treturn\n1078\t\t\t}\n1079\t\t\tif n == 0 {\n1080\t\t\t\tbreak\n1081\t\t\t}\n1082\t\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:n]\n1083\t\n1084\t\t\t// Verify HMAC\n1085\t\t\thmacOffset := blockIndex * crypto.HMACSize\n1086\t\t\tif hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize > len(hmacTable) {\n1087\t\t\t\treturn\n1088\t\t\t}\n1089\t\t\texpectedHMAC := hmacTable[hmacOffset : hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize]\n1090\t\n1091\t\t\tmac := hmac.New(sha256.New, decryptor.HMACKey())\n1092\t\t\tindexBytes := make([]byte, 4)\n1093\t\t\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(indexBytes, uint32(blockIndex))\n1094\t\t\tmac.Write(indexBytes)\n1095\t\t\tmac.Write(encryptedBuf)\n1096\t\t\tcomputed := mac.Sum(nil)\n1097\t\n1098\t\t\tif !hmac.Equal(computed, expectedHMAC) {\n1099\t\t\t\treturn\n1100\t\t\t}\n1101\t\n1102\t\t\t// Decrypt\n1103\t\t\tdecrypted := make([]byte, n)\n1104\t\t\tctr := makeCounter(armorMeta.IV, uint32(blockIndex))\n1105\t\t\tstream := cipher.NewCTR(decryptor.CipherBlock(), ctr)\n1106\t\t\tstream.XORKeyStream(decrypted, encryptedBuf)\n1107\t\n1108\t\t\t// Write to client\n1109\t\t\tw.Write(decrypted)\n1110\t\t}\n1111\t}\n1112\t\n1113\t// handleShareRangeRequest handles range requests for share endpoint.\n1114\tfunc (s *Server) handleShareRangeRequest(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, token *presign.Token, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, rangeHeader string) {\n1115\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1116\t\tplaintextSize := armorMeta.PlaintextSize\n1117\t\n1118\t\t// Parse range header\n1119\t\tstart, end, err := parseRangeHeader(rangeHeader, plaintextSize)\n1120\t\tif err != nil {\n1121\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid range\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n1122\t\t\treturn\n1123\t\t}\n1124\t\n1125\t\t// Translate range to encrypted blocks\n1126\t\ttranslation, err := crypto.TranslateRange(start, end, plaintextSize, http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1129\t\t\treturn\n1130\t\t}\n1131\t\n1132\t\t// Fetch encrypted blocks and HMAC table in parallel\n1133\t\tvar encrypted, hmacTable []byte\n1134\t\n1135\t\tg, gctx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)\n1136\t\n1137\t\tg.Go(func() error {\n1138\t\t\tencryptedBody, err := s.backend.GetRange(gctx, token.Bucket, token.Key, translation.DataOffset, translation.DataLength)\n1139\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1140\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch encrypted blocks: %w\", err)\n1141\t\t\t}\n1142\t\t\tdefer translation.HMACLength)\n1153\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1154\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch HMAC table: %w\", err)\n1155\t\t\t}\n1156\t\t\tdefer hmacBody.Close()\n1157\t\n1158\t\t\thmacTable, g.Wait(); err != nil {\n1166\t\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1167\t\t\treturn\n1168\t\t}\n1169\t\n1170\t\t// Decrypt range\n1171\t\tplaintext, err := http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1174\t\t\treturn\n1175\t\t}\n1176\t\n1177\t\t// Set response headers\n1178\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", plaintextSize))\n1180\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusPartialContent)\n1181\t\tw.Write(plaintext)\n1182\t}\n1183\t\n1184\tfunc min64(a, b int64) int64 {\n1185\t\tif a < b {\n1186\t\t\treturn a\n1187\t\t}\n1188\t\treturn b\n1189\t}\n1190\t\n1191\t// makeCounter creates a 16-byte counter value from the IV and block index.\n1192\tfunc makeCounter(iv []byte, blockIndex uint32) []byte {\n1193\t\tcounter := make([]byte, 16)\n1194\t\tcopy(counter[0:12], iv[0:12])\n1195\t\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(counter[12:16], blockIndex)\n1196\t\treturn counter\n1197\t}\n1198\t\n1199\t// parseRangeHeader parses a Range header like \"bytes=0-1023\".\n1200\tfunc parseRangeHeader(header string, totalSize int64) (start, end int64, err error) {\n1201\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(header, \"bytes=\") {\n1202\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n1203\t\t}\n1204\t\n1205\t\trangeSpec := strings.TrimPrefix(header, \"bytes=\")\n1206\t\n1207\t\tif strings.Contains(rangeSpec, \",\") {\n1208\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"multiple ranges not supported\")\n1209\t\t}\n1210\t\n1211\t\tparts := strings.Split(rangeSpec, \"-\")\n1212\t\tif len(parts) It mirrors manifest.Entry but is defined here to avoid an\n39\t// import cycle between the handlers and manifest packages.\n40\ttype ManifestEntry struct {\n41\t\tPlaintextSize int64\n42\t\tContentType   string\n43\t\tETag          string\n44\t\tLastModified  time.Time\n45\t\tIV            []byte\n46\t\tWrappedDEK    []byte\n47\t\tBlockSize     int\n48\t}\n49\t\n50\t// ManifestRecorder records successful S3 write operations in the manifest\n51\t// index for fast in-memory lookup and enqueues them for async B2 persistence\n52\t// as delta files. Implementations must be safe for concurrent use.\n53\ttype ManifestRecorder interface {\n54\t\t// RecordPut records a successful PutObject or CompleteMultipartUpload.\n55\t\tRecordPut(bucket, key string, size int64, sha256Hex string, iv, wrappedDEK []byte, blockSize int, contentType, etag string)\n56\t\t// RecordDelete records a successful DeleteObject.\n57\t\tRecordDelete(bucket, key string)\n58\t\t// Lookup returns manifest metadata for bucket/key, or (nil, false) if not\n59\t\t// tracked. Used to serve HeadObject and ListObjectVersions from memory\n60\t\t// without a B2 round-trip.\n61\t\tLookup(bucket, key string) (*ManifestEntry, bool)\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\t// Handlers contains all S3 operation handlers.\n65\ttype Handlers struct {\n66\t\tconfig      *config.Config\n67\t\tbackend     backend.Backend\n68\t\tcache       *backend.MetadataCache\n69\t\tfooterCache *backend.FooterCache\n70\t\tlistCache   *backend.ListCache\n71\t\tkeyManager  *keymanager.KeyManager\n72\t\tprovenance  ProvenanceRecorder\n73\t\tmanifest    ManifestRecorder\n74\t}\n75\t\n76\t// New creates a new Handlers instance.\n77\tfunc New(cfg *config.Config, be backend.Backend, cache *backend.MetadataCache, footerCache *backend.FooterCache, km *keymanager.KeyManager, listCache *backend.ListCache) *Handlers {\n78\t\treturn &Handlers{\n79\t\t\tconfig:      cfg,\n80\t\t\tbackend:     be,\n81\t\t\tcache:       cache,\n82\t\t\tfooterCache: footerCache,\n83\t\t\tlistCache:
