CreateMultipartUpload handles S3 CreateMultipartUpload with ARMOR encryption.\n1833\t// It generates a DEK and IV, wraps the DEK, and stores the state in B2.\n1834\tfunc (h *Handlers) CreateMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n1835\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1836\t\n1837\t\t// Get the appropriate MEK for this object key\n1838\t\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\n1839\t\tif err != nil {\n1840\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n1841\t\t\treturn\n1842\t\t}\n1843\t\n1844\t\t// Generate DEK and IV for this upload\n1845\t\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n1846\t\tif err != nil {\n1847\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1848\t\t\treturn\n1849\t\t}\n1850\t\n1851\t\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n1852\t\tif err != nil {\n1853\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate IV: %v\", err), 500)\n1854\t\t\treturn\n1855\t\t}\n1856\t\n1857\t\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n1858\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\n1859\t\tif err != nil {\n1860\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1861\t\t\treturn\n1862\t\t}\n1863\t\n1864\t\t// Get content type\n1865\t\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n1866\t\tif contentType == \"\" {\n1867\t\t\tcontentType = \"application/octet-stream\"\n1868\t\t}\n1869\t\n1870\t\t// Initiate multipart upload with B2 (no ARMOR metadata yet - that comes on completion)\n1871\t\tuploadID, err := h.backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, nil)\n1872\t\tif err != nil {\n1873\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n1874\t\t\treturn\n1875\t\t}\n1876\t\n1877\t\t// Save multipart state to B2\n1878\t\tstate := &backend.MultipartState{\n1879\t\t\tUploadID:       uploadID,\n1880\t\t\tBucket:         bucket,\n1881\t\t\tKey:            key,\n1882\t\t\tIV:             iv,\n1883\t\t\tWrappedDEK:     wrappedDEK,\n1884\t\t\tBlockSize:      h.config.BlockSize,\n1885\t\t\tCreated:        time.Now(),\n1886\t\t\tContentType:    contentType,\n1887\t\t\tKeyID:          keyID,\n1888\t\t\tEncryptedBytes: 0,\n1889\t\t\tPartHMACs:      make(map[int]string),\n1890\t\t\tPartSizes:      make(map[int]int64),\n1891\t\t}\n1892\t\n1893\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n1894\t\tif err := manager.SaveState(ctx, state); err != nil {\n1895\t\t\t// Try to abort the upload on state save failure\n1896\t\t\th.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID)\n1897\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to save multipart state: %v\", err), 500)\n1898\t\t\treturn\n1899\t\t}\n1900\t\n1901\t\t// Build XML response\n1902\t\ttype InitiateMultipartUploadResult struct {\n1903\t\t\tXMLName  xml.Name `xml:\"InitiateMultipartUploadResult\"`\n1904\t\t\tXmlns    string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n1905\t\t\tBucket   string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n1906\t\t\tKey      string   `xml:\"Key\"`\n1907\t\t\tUploadID string   `xml:\"UploadId\"`\n1908\t\t}\n1909\t\n1910\t\tresult := InitiateMultipartUploadResult{\n1911\t\t\tXmlns:    \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n1912\t\t\tBucket:   bucket,\n1913\t\t\tKey:      key,\n1914\t\t\tUploadID: uploadID,\n1915\t\t}\n1916\t\n1917\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n1918\t\tif err != nil {\n1919\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n1920\t\t\treturn\n1921\t\t}\n1922\t\n1923\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n1924\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1925\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n1926\t\tw.Write(output)\n1927\t}\n1928\t\n1929\t// UploadPart handles S3 UploadPart with encryption.\n1930\t// Each part is encrypted with a CTR counter offset based on cumulative encrypted bytes.\n1931\tfunc (h *Handlers) UploadPart(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n1932\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1933\t\n1934\t\t// Parse part number\n1935\t\tpartNumberStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"partNumber\")\n1936\t\tif partNumberStr == \"\" {\n1937\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Missing partNumber\", 400)\n1938\t\t\treturn\n1939\t\t}\n1940\t\tpartNumber, err := strconv.ParseInt(partNumberStr, 10, 32)\n1941\t\tif err != nil || partNumber < 1 || partNumber > 10000 {\n1942\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Invalid partNumber\", 400)\n1943\t\t\treturn\n1944\t\t}\n1945\t\n1946\t\t// Load multipart state\n1947\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n1948\t\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n1949\t\tif err != nil {\n1950\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n1951\t\t\treturn\n1952\t\t}\n1953\t\n1954\t\t// Verify bucket and key match\n1955\t\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n1956\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n1957\t\t\treturn\n1958\t\t}\n1959\t\n1960\t\t// Read plaintext part\n1961\t\tplaintext, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n1962\t\tif err != nil {\n1963\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n1964\t\t\treturn\n1965\t\t}\n1966\t\n1967\t\tplaintextSize := int64(len(plaintext))\n1968\t\n1969\t\t// Get the MEK for this upload using the key ID from state\n1970\t\tmek, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(state.KeyID)\n1971\t\tif err != nil {\n1972\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get decryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n1973\t\t\treturn\n1974\t\t}\n1975\t\n1976\t\t// Unwrap DEK\n1977\t\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(mek, state.WrappedDEK)\n1978\t\tif err != nil {\n1979\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1980\t\t\treturn\n1981\t\t}\n1982\t\n1983\t\t// Calculate starting block index for CTR counter\n1984\t\t// Each block is blockSize bytes, so counter = encryptedBytes / blockSize\n1985\t\tstartBlockIndex := uint32(state.EncryptedBytes / int64(state.BlockSize))\n1986\t\n1987\t\t// Create encryptor with the part's starting counter\n1988\t\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptorWithCounter(dek, state.IV, state.BlockSize, startBlockIndex)\n1989\t\tif err != nil {\n1990\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n1991\t\t\treturn\n1992\t\t}\n1993\t\n1994\t\t// Encrypt the part\n1995\t\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(plaintext)\n1996\t\tif err != nil {\n1997\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encrypt: %v\", err), 500)\n1998\t\t\treturn\n1999\t\t}\n2000\t\n2001\t\t// Derive HMAC key\n2002\t\thmacKey := crypto.DeriveHMACKey(dek)\n2003\t\n2004\t\t// Compute block HMACs\n2005\t\tblockHMACs, err := backend.ComputeBlockHMACs(encrypted, state.BlockSize, hmacKey, startBlockIndex)\n2006\t\tif err != nil {\n2007\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to compute HMACs: %v\", err), 500)\n2008\t\t\treturn\n2009\t\t}\n2010\t\n2011\t\t// Upload encrypted part to B2\n2012\t\tetag, err := h.backend.UploadPart(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, int32(partNumber), bytes.NewReader(encrypted), int64(len(encrypted)))\n2013\t\tif err != nil {\n2014\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to upload part: %v\", err), 500)\n2015\t\t\treturn\n2016\t\t}\n2017\t\n2018\t\t// Update state with cumulative bytes and part HMACs\n2019\t\tstate.EncryptedBytes += int64(len(encrypted))\n2020\t\tstate.PartHMACs[int(partNumber)] = backend.EncodeHMACToBase64(blockHMACs)\n2021\t\tstate.PartSizes[int(partNumber)] = plaintextSize\n2022\t\n2023\t\tif err := manager.SaveState(ctx, state); err != nil {\n2024\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to update multipart state: %v\", err), 500)\n2025\t\t\treturn\n2026\t\t}\n2027\t\n2028\t\t// Return ETag - suppress unused variable warning\n2029\t\t_ = hmacTable\n2030\t\n2031\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", etag)\n2032\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n2033\t}\n2034\t\n2035\t// CompleteMultipartUpload handles S3 CompleteMultipartUpload.\n2036\t// It assembles the parts in B2 and stores the HMAC table as a sidecar.\n2037\tfunc (h *Handlers) CompleteMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n2038\t\tctx := r.Context()\n2039\t\n2040\t\t// Load multipart state\n2041\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n2042\t\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n2043\t\tif err != nil {\n2044\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n2045\t\t\treturn\n2046\t\t}\n2047\t\n2048\t\t// Verify bucket and key match\n2049\t\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n2050\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n2051\t\t\treturn\n2052\t\t}\n2053\t\n2054\t\t// Parse the CompleteMultipartUpload request XML\n2055\t\ttype Part struct {\n2056\t\t\tPartNumber int    `xml:\"PartNumber\"`\n2057\t\t\tETag       string `xml:\"ETag\"`\n2058\t\t}\n2059\t\n2060\t\ttype CompleteMultipartUploadReq struct {\n2061\t\t\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"CompleteMultipartUpload\"`\n2062\t\t\tParts   []Part   `xml:\"Part\"`\n2063\t\t}\n2064\t\n2065\t\tvar completeReq CompleteMultipartUploadReq\n2066\t\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n2067\t\tif err != nil {\n2068\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n2069\t\t\treturn\n2070\t\t}\n2071\t\n2072\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &completeReq); err != nil {\n2073\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"MalformedXML\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse XML: %v\", err), 400)\n2074\t\t\treturn\n2075\t\t}\n2076\t\n2077\t\tif len(completeReq.Parts) == 0 {\n2078\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"No parts specified\", 400)\n2079\t\t\treturn\n2080\t\t}\n2081\t\n2082\t\t// Convert to backend.CompletedPart\n2083\t\tparts := make([]backend.CompletedPart, len(completeReq.Parts))\n2084\t\tfor i, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n2085\t\t\tparts[i] = backend.CompletedPart{\n2086\t\t\t\tPartNumber: int32(p.PartNumber),\n2087\t\t\t\tETag:       p.ETag,\n2088\t\t\t}\n2089\t\t}\n2090\t\n2091\t\t// Calculate total plaintext size\n2092\t\tvar totalPlaintextSize int64\n2093\t\tfor _, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n2094\t\t\tif size, ok := state.PartSizes[p.PartNumber]; ok {\n2095\t\t\t\ttotalPlaintextSize += size\n2096\t\t\t}\n2097\t\t}\n2098\t\n2099\t\t// Assemble all block HMACs in order\n2100\t\tvar allBlockHMACs [][]byte\n2101\t\tfor _, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n2102\t\t\tif hmacsBase64, ok := state.PartHMACs[p.PartNumber]; ok {\n2103\t\t\t\thmacs, err := backend.DecodeHMACFromBase64(hmacsBase64)\n2104\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n2105\t\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decode HMACs for part %d: %v\", p.PartNumber, err), 500)\n2106\t\t\t\t\treturn\n2107\t\t\t\t}\n2108\t\t\t\tallBlockHMACs = append(allBlockHMACs, hmacs...)\n2109\t\t\t}\n2110\t\t}\n2111\t\n2112\t\t// Complete the multipart upload in B2\n2113\t\tetag, err := h.backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, parts)\n2114\t\tif err != nil {\n2115\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to complete multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n2116\t\t\treturn\n2117\t\t}\n2118\t\n2119\t\t// Store HMAC table as sidecar\n2120\t\tif err := manager.SaveHMACTable(ctx, key, allBlockHMACs, state.BlockSize); err != nil {\n2121\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to save HMAC table: %v\", err), 500)\n2122\t\t\treturn\n2123\t\t}\n2124\t\n2125\t\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256 (we don't have the full plaintext, so use a placeholder)\n2126\t\t// In a full implementation, we'd track SHA during upload\n2127\t\tplaintextSHA := sha256.Sum256([]byte{})\n2128\t\n2129\t\t// Build ARMOR metadata and update via CopyObject\n2130\t\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n2131\t\t\tVersion:       1,\n2132\t\t\tBlockSize:     state.BlockSize,\n2133\t\t\tPlaintextSize: totalPlaintextSize,\n2134\t\t\tContentType:   state.ContentType,\n2135\t\t\tIV:            state.IV,\n2136\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    state.WrappedDEK,\n2137\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n2138\t\t\tETag:          etag,\n2139\t\t\tKeyID:         state.KeyID,\n2140\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n2141\t\n2142\t\t// Add multipart flag to indicate HMAC table is external\n2143\t\tmeta[\"x-amz-meta-armor-multipart\"] = \"true\"\n2144\t\n2145\t\t// Update metadata via CopyObject\n2146\t\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, bucket, key, bucket, key, meta, true); err != nil {\n2147\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to update metadata: %v\", err), 500)\n2148\t\t\treturn\n2149\t\t}\n2150\t\n2151\t\t// Clean up multipart state\n2152\t\tmanager.DeleteState(ctx, uploadID)\n2153\t\n2154\t\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\n2155\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n2156\t\t\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, totalPlaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), state.IV, state.WrappedDEK, state.BlockSize, state.ContentType, etag)\n2157\t\t}\n2158\t\n2159\t\t// Record provenance for the multipart upload\n2160\t\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\n2161\t\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), \"multipart\")\n2162\t\t}\n2163\t\n2164\t\t// Build XML response\n2165\t\ttype CompleteMultipartUploadResult struct {\n2166\t\t\tXMLName  xml.Name `xml:\"CompleteMultipartUploadResult\"`\n2167\t\t\tXmlns    string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n2168\t\t\tLocation string   `xml:\"Location\"`\n2169\t\t\tBucket   string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n2170\t\t\tKey      string   `xml:\"Key\"`\n2171\t\t\tETag     string   `xml:\"ETag\"`\n2172\t\t}\n2173\t\n2174\t\tresult := CompleteMultipartUploadResult{\n2175\t\t\tXmlns:    \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n2176\t\t\tLocation: fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s\", bucket, key),\n2177\t\t\tBucket:   bucket,\n2178\t\t\tKey:      key,\n2179\t\t\tETag:     etag,\n2180\t\t}\n2181\t\n2182\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n2183\t\tif err != nil {\n2184\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n2185\t\t\treturn\n2186\t\t}\n2187\t\n2188\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n2189\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n2190\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n2191\t\tw.Write(output)\n2192\t}\n2193\t\n2194\t// AbortMultipartUpload handles S3 AbortMultipartUpload.\n2195\t// It deletes the multipart state and forwards the abort to B2.\n2196\tfunc (h *Handlers) AbortMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n2197\t\tctx := r.Context()\n2198\t\n2199\t\t// Load multipart state to verify it exists\n2200\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n2201\t\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n2202\t\tif err != nil {\n2203\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n2204\t\t\treturn\n2205\t\t}\n2206\t\n2207\t\t// Verify bucket and key match\n2208\t\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n2209\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n2210\t\t\treturn\n2211\t\t}\n2212\t\n2213\t\t// Forward abort to B2\n2214\t\tif err := h.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID); err != nil {\n2215\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to abort multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n2216\t\t\treturn\n2217\t\t}\n2218\t\n2219\t\t// Clean up multipart state\n2220\t\tmanager.DeleteState(ctx, uploadID)\n2221\t\n2222\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n2223\t}\n2224\t\n2225\t// ListParts handles S3 ListParts operation.\n2226\t// It forwards to B2 and adjusts part sizes to plaintext sizes.\n2227\tfunc (h *Handlers) ListParts(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n2228\t\tctx := r.Context()\n2229\t\n2230\t\t// Load multipart state to get plaintext sizes\n2231\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n2232\t\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n2233\t\tif err != nil {\n2234\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n2235\t\t\treturn\n2236\t\t}\n2237\t\n2238\t\t// Verify bucket and key match\n2239\t\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n2240\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n2241\t\t\treturn\n2242\t\t}\n2243\t\n2244\t\t// Forward to B2\n2245\t\tresult, err := h.backend.ListParts(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID)\n2246\t\tif err != nil {\n2247\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list parts: %v\", err), 500)\n2248\t\t\treturn\n2249\t\t}\n2250\t\n2251\t\t// Build XML response with plaintext sizes\n2252\t\ttype Part struct {\n2253\t\t\tPartNumber   int    `xml:\"PartNumber\"`\n2254\t\t\tETag         string `xml:\"ETag\"`\n2255\t\t\tSize         int64  `xml:\"Size\"`\n2256\t\t\tLastModified string `xml:\"LastModified\"`\n2257\t\t}\n2258\t\n2259\t\ttype ListPartsResult struct {\n2260\t\t\tXMLName              xml.Name `xml:\"ListPartsResult\"`\n2261\t\t\tXmlns                string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n2262\t\t\tBucket               string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n2263\t\t\tKey                  string   `xml:\"Key\"`\n2264\t\t\tUploadID             string   `xml:\"UploadId\"`\n2265\t\t\tStorageClass         string   `xml:\"StorageClass\"`\n2266\t\t\tPartNumberMarker     int      `xml:\"PartNumberMarker\"`\n2267\t\t\tNextPartNumberMarker int      `xml:\"NextPartNumberMarker\"`\n2268\t\t\tMaxParts             int      `xml:\"MaxParts\"`\n2269\t\t\tIsTruncated          bool     `xml:\"IsTruncated\"`\n2270\t\t\tParts                []Part   `xml:\"Part\"`\n2271\t\t}\n2272\t\n2273\t\tresp := ListPartsResult{\n2274\t\t\tXmlns:                \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n2275\t\t\tBucket:               bucket,\n2276\t\t\tKey:                  key,\n2277\t\t\tUploadID:             uploadID,\n2278\t\t\tStorageClass:         \"STANDARD\",\n2279\t\t\tPartNumberMarker:     result.NextPartNumberMarker,\n2280\t\t\tNextPartNumberMarker: result.NextPartNumberMarker,\n2281\t\t\tIsTruncated:          result.IsTruncated,\n2282\t\t}\n2283\t\n2284\t\tfor _, part := range result.Parts {\n2285\t\t\t// Use plaintext size from state if available, otherwise use reported size\n2286\t\t\tplaintextSize := part.Size\n2287\t\t\tif size, ok := state.PartSizes[int(part.PartNumber)]; ok {\n2288\t\t\t\tplaintextSize = size\n2289\t\t\t}\n2290\t\n2291\t\t\tresp.Parts = append(resp.Parts, Part{\n2292\t\t\t\tPartNumber:   int(part.PartNumber),\n2293\t\t\t\tETag:         part.ETag,\n2294\t\t\t\tSize:         plaintextSize,\n2295\t\t\t\tLastModified: \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n2302\t\t\treturn\n2303\t\t}\n2304\t\n2305\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n2306\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n2307\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n2308\t\tw.Write(output)\n2309\t}\n2310\t\n2311\t// {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_40806b8899cb4105b2e2ee08","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# ARMOR Error Response Documentation\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tARMOR provides S3-compatible error responses for all authentication and request failures. This document describes the error response format, error codes, and performance characteristics.\n6\t\n7\t## Error Response Format\n8\t\n9\tAll error responses follow the S3 XML error format with consistent headers:\n10\t\n11\t### HTTP Headers\n12\t\n13\t```http\n14\tContent-Type: application/xml\n15\tStatus: 403 Forbidden (for authentication errors)\n16\t```\n17\t\n18\t### Response Body\n19\t\n20\t```xml\n21\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n22\t<Error>\n23\t  <Code>ErrorCode</Code>\n24\t  <Message>Human-readable error description</Message>\n25\t</Error>\n26\t```\n27\t\n28\t## Authentication Error Codes\n29\t\n30\t### Invalid Credentials\n31\t\n32\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n33\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n34\t| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records. | Access key not found in credentials store |\n35\t| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | 403 | The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided. | Secret key is incorrect or signature is invalid |\n36\t| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Missing authentication token. | Authorization header is completely missing |\n37\t| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Invalid credential format. | Credential string has insufficient parts (expected 5 parts) |\n38\t\n39\t### Malformed Authorization Headers\n40\t\n41\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n42\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n43\t| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | The authorization header is malformed. | Required components missing (Credential, SignedHeaders, or Signature) |\n44\t| `InvalidAlgorithm` | 403 | The authorization header algorithm is not supported. | Algorithm is not AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 |\n45\t| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Access Denied. | Generic authentication failure |\n46\t\n47\t### Timing Issues\n48\t\n49\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n50\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n51\t| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Request has expired. | Request timestamp is outside the allowed window (±15 minutes) |\n52\t| `MissingDateHeader` | 403 | Missing required header: x-amz-date. | X-Amz-Date header is missing |\n53\t\n54\t## Malformed Signature Scenarios\n55\t\n56\tARMOR validates signature format and provides specific error codes:\n57\t\n58\t| Scenario | Error Code | Performance |\n59\t|----------|------------|-------------|\n60\t| Non-hex signature characters | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n61\t| Too short signature (< 32 bytes) | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n62\t| Empty signature | `IncompleteSignature` | < 50ms |\n63\t| Random characters in signature | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n64\t\n65\t## Performance Guarantees\n66\t\n67\tAll authentication rejection scenarios respond within strict time limits:\n68\t\n69\t| Test Type | Target | Actual | Environment |\n70\t|-----------|--------|--------|-------------|\n71\t| Unit test rejections | < 100ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n72\t| Integration test rejections | < 500ms | < 50ms | Real server |\n73\t| Malformed signature rejections | < 50ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n74\t\n75\t### Performance Test Coverage\n76\t\n77\tThe test suite includes performance verification for all rejection scenarios:\n78\t\n79\t1. **TestInvalidCredentialRejection/Rejection_happens_quickly** - Verifies < 100ms response time\n80\t2. **TestMalformedSignatureRejection/Rejection_happens_quickly** - Verifies < 50ms response time for malformed signatures\n81\t\n82\t## Error Message Quality\n83\t\n84\tAll error responses include:\n85\t\n86\t1. **Specific Error Code** - Identifies the exact problem (e.g., `InvalidAccessKeyId`, `SignatureDoesNotMatch`)\n87\t2. **Meaningful Message** - Human-readable description of the problem\n88\t3. **XML Format** - S3-compatible XML structure with proper escaping\n89\t\n90\t### Message Validation\n91\t\n92\tTest suite verifies that:\n93\t- Error messages are never empty\n94\t- Messages are at least 10 characters long\n95\t- Messages contain relevant keywords (authentication, signature, credential, algorithm, header, aws4)\n96\t- XML is properly escaped to prevent injection\n97\t\n98\t## Response Consistency\n99\t\n100\tAll error responses maintain consistency:\n101\t\n102\t### Headers\n103\t- Always return `Content-Type: application/xml`\n104\t- Always return appropriate HTTP status code (403 for auth errors)\n105\t- Response body is never empty\n106\t\n107\t### Structure\n108\t- XML declaration with encoding: `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`\n109\t- Root element: `<Error>`\n110\t- Two child elements: `<Code>` and `<Message>`\n111\t- Proper XML escaping for special characters\n112\t\n113\t## Test Coverage Summary\n114\t\n115\tThe ARMOR test suite includes comprehensive coverage for rejection scenarios:\n116\t\n117\t### Unit Tests (`invalid_credential_test.go`)\n118\t- 12 test scenarios covering:\n119\t  - Invalid AWS credentials\n120\t  - Malformed signatures  \n121\t  - Missing authentication headers\n122\t  - Malformed authorization headers\n123\t  - Insufficient credential parts\n124\t  - Missing required components (SignedHeaders, Signature, date)\n125\t  - Expired requests\n126\t  - Performance validation\n127\t\n128\t### Unit Tests (`malformed_signature_test.go`)\n129\t- 20+ test scenarios covering:\n130\t  - Garbage signature strings (non-hex, too short, empty, random chars)\n131\t  - Invalid signature formats (missing algorithm, wrong algorithm, missing components)\n132\t  - Partial signatures (missing components)\n133\t  - Error message quality\n134\t  - Performance validation\n135\t\n136\t### Integration Tests (`invalid_credential_integration_test.go`)\n137\t- Real server tests with actual HTTP client\n138\t- Performance validation under realistic conditions\n139\t- End-to-end verification of error responses\n140\t\n141\t### Headers Consistency (`error_response_test.go`)\n142\t- Verifies consistent headers across all rejection types\n143\t- Validates Content-Type header\n144\t- Ensures proper XML structure\n145\t\n146\t## Examples\n147\t\n148\t### Example 1: Invalid Access Key\n149\t\n150\t**Request:**\n151\t```http\n152\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n153\tHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n154\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=INVALIDKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=abc123...\n155\tX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n156\t```\n157\t\n158\t**Response:**\n159\t```http\n160\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n161\tContent-Type: application/xml\n162\t\n163\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n164\t<Error>\n165\t  <Code>InvalidAccessKeyId</Code>\n166\t  <Message>The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records.</Message>\n167\t</Error>\n168\t```\n169\t\n170\t### Example 2: Missing Authentication\n171\t\n172\t**Request:**\n173\t```http\n174\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n175\tHost: encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n184\t<Error>\n185\t  <Code>MissingAuthenticationToken</Code>\n186\t  <Message>Missing authentication token.</Message>\n187\t</Error>\n188\t```\n189\t\n190\t### Example 3: Malformed Signature\n191\t\n192\t**Request:**\n193\t```http\n194\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n195\tHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n196\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=!@#$%^&*()\n197\tX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n198\t```\n199\t\n200\t**Response:**\n201\t```http\n202\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n203\tContent-Type: application/xml\n204\t\n205\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n206\t<Error>\n207\t  <Code>SignatureDoesNotMatch</Code>\n208\t  <Message>The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided.</Message>\n209\t</Error>\n210\t```\n211\t\n212\t## Implementation\n213\t\n214\tError responses are generated by two implementations:\n215\t\n216\t1. **Server Handler** (`internal/server/server.go:796-805`)\n217\t   ```go\n218\t   func (s *Server) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n219\t       w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n220\t       w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n221\t       // XML generation with proper escaping\n222\t   }\n223\t   ```\n224\t\n225\t2. **Handlers Package** (`internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:2695-2704`)\n226\t   ```go\n227\t   func (h *Handlers) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n228\t       w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n229\t       w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n230\t       // XML generation with proper escaping\n231\t   }\n232\t   ```\n233\t\n234\tBoth implementations ensure:\n235\t- Consistent Content-Type header\n236\t- Proper XML escaping to prevent injection\n237\t- S3-compatible format\n238\t\n239\t## Testing\n240\t\n241\tTo run the error This specification consolidates findings from comprehensive header consistency verification across multiple verification beads:\n10\t\n11\t- **Comprehensive header specification** (bf-2n6273) - This document\n12\t- **Header consistency verification** (bf-649uw6) - Identified admin interface inconsistencies\n13\t- **Content-Type consistency verification** (bf-4bwxtc) - Verified `application/xml` consistency\n14\t- **HTTP status code consistency verification** (bf-o7eo21) - Verified HTTP 403 consistency\n15\t- **Authentication rejection response headers** (bf-5ppsfh) - Auth rejection documentation\n16\t- **Invalid AWS credentials rejection testing** (bf-58oib3) - Invalid credential testing\n17\t\n18\tThis specification provides:\n19\t1. **Required headers** for all error responses\n20\t2. **Expected values** for each header\n21\t3. **Allowed variations** (where applicable)\n22\t4. **Examples** of correct error responses\n23\t5. **Discovered inconsistencies** and remediation recommendations\n24\t6. **Performance requirements** and testing guidelines\n25\t\n26\t## Scope\n27\t\n28\tThis specification applies to **all error responses** from ARMOR, including:\n29\t\n30\t1. Authentication failures (HTTP 403)\n31\t2. Authorization failures (HTTP 403)\n32\t3. Input validation errors (HTTP 400, 405)\n33\t4. Resource not found errors (HTTP 404)\n34\t5. Conditional request errors (HTTP 412)\n35\t6. Internal server errors (HTTP 500)\n36\t7. Service unavailable errors (HTTP 503)\n37\t\n38\t## Required Headers\n39\t\n40\t### All Error Responses\n41\t\n42\t**All error responses MUST include the following header:**\n43\t\n44\t```\n45\tContent-Type: application/xml\n46\t```\n47\t\n48\t**Specification:**\n49\t- **Header Name:** `Content-Type`\n50\t- **Required Value:** `application/xml`\n51\t- **Case Sensitivity:** Header names are case-insensitive per HTTP/1.1 specification\n52\t- **Allowed Variations:** None - must be exactly `application/xml`\n53\t- **Charset:** Optional; if specified, must be `application/xml; charset=utf-8`\n54\t- **Position:** Must be set before calling `WriteHeader()`\n55\t\n56\t**Implementation Detail:**\n57\tAll error responses are generated by one of two identical `writeError` functions:\n58\t- `internal/server/server.go:writeError` (lines 796-805)\n59\t- `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:writeError` (lines 2695-2704)\n60\t\n61\tBoth implementations set:\n62\t```go\n63\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n64\tw.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n65\t```\n66\t\n67\t### CORS Headers (Authentication Rejections Only)\n68\t\n69\t**Authentication and authorization rejection responses (HTTP 403) MUST include CORS headers:**\n70\t\n71\t```\n72\tAccess-Control-Allow-Origin: *\n73\tAccess-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\n74\tAccess-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\n75\t```\n76\t\n77\t**Specification:**\n78\t- **Scope:** Only HTTP 403 responses for authentication/authorization failures\n79\t- **Header Order:** Not significant (may vary between responses)\n80\t- **Allow-Origin Value:** Must be `*` (wildcard)\n81\t- **Allow-Methods Value:** Must include: `GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS`\n82\t- **Allow-Headers Value:** Must include: `Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length`\n83\t\n84\t**Note:** These CORS headers are added by middleware for authentication endpoints. Other error types (400, 404, 500, etc.) do not include CORS headers.\n85\t\n86\t## HTTP Status Codes\n87\t\n88\t### Status Code Assignment by Error Category\n89\t\n90\t| HTTP Status | Error Category | Example Error Codes |\n91\t|-------------|----------------|---------------------|\n92\t| **400** | Client Input Errors | `InvalidRequest`, `InvalidRange`, `MalformedXML` |\n93\t| **403** | Authentication/Authorization | `MissingAuthenticationToken`, `InvalidAccessKeyId`, `SignatureDoesNotMatch`, `AccessDenied` |\n94\t| **404** | Resource Not Found | `NoSuchKey`, `NoSuchBucket`, `NoSuchUpload` |\n95\t| **405** | Method Not Allowed | `MethodNotAllowed` |\n96\t| **412** | Conditional Request Failed | `PreconditionFailed` |\n97\t| **500** | Internal Server Error | `InternalError` |\n98\t| **503** | Service Unavailable | `ServiceUnavailable` |\n99\t\n100\t### Status Code Consistency Requirements\n101\t\n102\t**Authentication/Authorization Errors (HTTP 403):**\n103\tAll of the following error codes MUST return HTTP 403:\n104\t\n105\t| Error Code | Trigger |\n106\t|------------|---------|\n107\t| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | Authorization header is missing |\n108\t| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | Access key does not exist |\n109\t| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | Signature validation failed |\n110\t| `InvalidAlgorithm` | Non-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 algorithm |\n111\t| `MissingDateHeader` | X-Amz-Date header missing |\n112\t| `RequestExpired` | Request outside 15-minute window |\n113\t| `IncompleteSignature` | Authorization header missing required fields |\n114\t| `InvalidCredential` | Invalid credential format |\n115\t| `InvalidDateFormat` | Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date |\n116\t| `AccessDenied` | ACL-based authorization rejection |\n117\t\n118\t**Verification:** All 10 authentication error codes have been verified to return HTTP 403 (see bf-o7eo21).\n119\t\n120\t## Response Body Format\n121\t\n122\t### Required XML Structure\n123\t\n124\tAll error responses MUST follow this exact XML structure:\n125\t\n126\t```xml\n127\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n128\t<Error>\n129\t  <Code>ERROR_CODE</Code>\n130\t  <Message>Error message description</Message>\n131\t</Error>\n132\t```\n133\t\n134\t**Specification:**\n135\t- **XML Declaration:** Required, must be `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`\n136\t- **Root Element:** Must be `<Error>`\n137\t- **Code Element:** Required, must contain error code (see Error Codes section)\n138\t- **Message Element:** Required, must contain human-readable error message\n139\t- **XML Escaping:** All special characters in `<Code>` and `<Message>` MUST be escaped\n140\t- **Whitespace:** Newline and spacing may vary but structure must be preserved\n141\t\n142\t**Allowed Variations:**\n143\t- Message content varies by error type and may include dynamic context (e.g., `\"Failed to encrypt: {error}\"`)\n144\t- Code values are predefined constants (see Error Codes section)\n145\t\n146\t### Error Code Casing\n147\t\n148\tAll error codes MUST use **PascalCase** (matching AWS S3 conventions):\n149\t\n150\t- ✅ Correct: `SignatureDoesNotMatch`, `InvalidAccessKeyId`\n151\t- ❌ Incorrect: `signatureDoesNotMatch`, `invalid-access-key-id`\n152\t\n153\t## Error Codes Reference\n154\t\n155\t### Authentication/Authorization Error Codes (HTTP 403)\n156\t\n157\t| Error Code | Message | When Returned |\n158\t|------------|---------|---------------|\n159\t| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | Missing Authentication Token | Authorization header is completely missing |\n160\t| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist | Access key not found in credentials store |\n161\t| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided | Secret key is incorrect or signature is invalid |\n162\t| `RequestExpired` | Request has expired | Request timestamp is outside the allowed window (±15 minutes) |\n163\t| `InvalidAlgorithm` | Only AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 is supported | Algorithm is not AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 |\n164\t| `IncompleteSignature` | Authorization header is missing required fields | Required components missing (Credential, SignedHeaders, or Signature) |\n165\t| `MissingDateHeader` | Missing X-Amz-Date header | X-Amz-Date header is missing |\n166\t| `InvalidDateFormat` | Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header | X-Amz-Date format is invalid |\n167\t| `InvalidCredential` | Invalid credential format | Credential string has insufficient parts (expected 5 parts) |\n168\t| `AccessDenied` | Access Denied | ACL-based access control rejection |\n169\t\n170\t### Client Input Error Codes (HTTP 400/405)\n171\t\n172\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message |\n173\t|------------|-------------|---------|\n174\t| `InvalidRequest` | 400 | Varies (e.g., \"Unsupported POST operation\") |\n175\t| `InvalidRange` | 400 | \"Invalid range: {details}\" |\n176\t| `MalformedXML` | 400 | \"Failed to parse XML: {details}\" |\n177\t| `MethodNotAllowed` | 405 | \"Method {method} not allowed\" |\n178\t\n179\t### Resource Not Found Error Codes (HTTP 404)\n180\t\n181\t| Error Code | Message |\n182\t|------------|---------|\n183\t| `NoSuchKey` | Object not found |\n184\t| `NoSuchBucket` | Bucket not found |\n185\t| `NoSuchUpload` | Multipart upload not found |\n186\t\n187\t### Conditional Request Error Codes (HTTP 412)\n188\t\n189\t| Error Code | Message |\n190\t|------------|---------|\n191\t| `PreconditionFailed` | Precondition failed |\n192\t\n193\t### Internal Error Codes (HTTP 500/503)\n194\t\n195\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message |\n196\t|------------|-------------|---------|\n197\t| `InternalError` | 500 | Varies (e.g., \"Failed to encrypt: {error}\") |\n198\t| `ServiceUnavailable` | 503 | Varies |\n199\t\n200\t## Examples by Rejection Scenario\n201\t\n202\t### Example 1: Missing Authentication Token\n203\t\n204\t**Request:**\n205\t```http\n206\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n207\tHost: armor.example.com\n208\t```\n209\t\n210\t**Response:**\n211\t```http\n212\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n213\tContent-Type: application/xml\n214\tAccess-Control-Allow-Origin: *\n215\tAccess-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\n216\tAccess-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\n217\t\n218\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n219\t<Error>\n220\t  <Code>MissingAuthenticationToken</Code>\n221\t  <Message>Missing Authentication Token</Message>\n222\t</Error>\n223\t```\n224\t\n225\t### Example 2: Invalid Access Key\n226\t\n227\t**Request:**\n228\t```http\n229\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n230\tHost: armor.example.com\n231\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=INVALIDKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=abc123...\n232\tX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n233\t```\n234\t\n235\t**Response:**\n236\t```http\n237\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n238\tContent-Type: application/xml\n239\tAccess-Control-Allow-Origin: *\n240\tAccess-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\n241\tAccess-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\n242\t\n243\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n244\t<Error>\n245\t  <Code>InvalidAccessKeyId</Code>\n246\t  <Message>The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist</Message>\n247\t</Error>\n248\t```\n249\t\n250\t### Example 3: Signature Does Not Match\n251\t\n252\t**Request:**\n253\t```http\n254\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n255\tHost: armor.example.com\n256\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=wrongsignature\n257\tX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n258\t```\n259\t\n260\t**Response:**\n261\t```http\n262\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n263\tContent-Type: application/xml\n264\tAccess-Control-Allow-Origin: *\n265\tAccess-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\n266\tAccess-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\n267\t\n268\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n269\t<Error>\n270\t  <Code>SignatureDoesNotMatch</Code>\n271\t  <Message>The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided</Message>\n272\t</Error>\n273\t```\n274\t\n275\t### Example 4: Expired Request\n276\t\n277\t**Request:**\n278\t```http\n279\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n280\tHost: armor.example.com\n281\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250601/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=abc123...\n282\tX-Amz-Date: 20250601T044805Z\n283\t```\n284\t\n285\t**Response:**\n286\t```http\n287\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n288\tContent-Type: application/xml\n289\tAccess-Control-Allow-Origin: *\n290\tAccess-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\n291\tAccess-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\n292\t\n293\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n294\t<Error>\n295\t  <Code>RequestExpired</Code>\n296\t  <Message>Request has expired</Message>\n297\t</Error>\n298\t```\n299\t\n300\t### Example 5: Resource Not Found (404 - No CORS Headers)\n301\t\n302\t**Request:**\n303\t```http\n304\tGET /test-bucket/nonexistent-key HTTP/1.1\n305\tHost: armor.example.com\n306\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=VALIDKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=validsignature...\n307\tX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n308\t```\n309\t\n310\t**Response:**\n311\t```http\n312\tHTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\n313\tContent-Type: application/xml\n314\t\n315\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n316\t<Error>\n317\t  <Code>NoSuchKey</Code>\n318\t  <Message>Object not found</Message>\n319\t</Error>\n320\t```\n321\t\n322\t**Note:** 404 responses do NOT include CORS headers (unlike 403 responses).\n323\t\n324\t### Example 6: Invalid Range Request (400)\n325\t\n326\t**Request:**\n327\t```http\n328\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n329\tHost: armor.example.com\n330\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=VALIDKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=validsignature...\n331\tX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n332\tRange: bytes=invalid\n333\t```\n334\t\n335\t**Response:**\n336\t```http\n337\tHTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request\n338\tContent-Type: application/xml\n339\t\n340\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n341\t<Error>\n342\t  <Code>InvalidRange</Code>\n343\t  <Message>Invalid range: {details}</Message>\n344\t</Error>\n345\t```\n346\t\n347\t### Example 7: Internal Server Error (500)\n348\t\n349\t**Request:**\n350\t```http\n351\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n352\tHost: armor.example.com\n353\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=VALIDKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=validsignature...\n354\tX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n355\t```\n356\t\n357\t**Response:**\n358\t```http\n359\tHTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error\n360\tContent-Type: application/xml\n361\t\n362\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n363\t<Error>\n364\t  <Code>InternalError</Code>\n365\t  <Message>Failed to encrypt: encryption error details</Message>\n366\t</Error>\n367\t```\n368\t\n369\t## Consistency Verification Results\n370\t\n371\t### Header Consistency Verification Summary\n372\t\n373\t| Aspect | Status | Details |\n374\t|--------|--------|---------|\n375\t| **Content-Type header consistency** | ✅ PASS | All error responses return `application/xml` |\n376\t| **HTTP status code consistency** | ✅ PASS | All authentication/authorization errors return 403 |\n377\t| **XML structure consistency** | ✅ PASS | All responses follow S3 XML error format |\n378\t| **Error code casing** | ✅ PASS | All codes use PascalCase |\n379\t| **XML escaping** | ✅ PASS | Special characters properly escaped |\n380\t\n381\t### Known Inconsistencies and Gaps\n382\t\n383\t#### 1. Code Duplication (Low Severity)\n384\t\n385\t**Issue:** Two identical `writeError` functions exist:\n386\t- `internal/server/server.go:writeError`\n387\t- `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:writeError`\n388\t\n389\t**Impact:** Maintenance risk if one is updated without the other\n390\t\n391\t**Current Status:** ✅ No functional inconsistency - both implementations are identical\n392\t\n393\t**Recommendation:** Consider extracting to a shared utility function to prevent drift\n394\t\n395\t**Tracking:** This is a code quality issue, not a functional inconsistency\n396\t\n397\t#### 2. CORS Headers Present Only on 403 Responses\n398\t\n399\t**Issue:** CORS headers are only present on HTTP 403 authentication/authorization responses\n400\t\n401\t**Impact:** None - this is expected behavior\n402\t\n403\t**Current Status:** ✅ Working as designed - CORS middleware only applies to auth endpoints\n404\t\n405\t**Recommendation:** No change needed - document this behavior clearly for consumers\n406\t\n407\t#### 3. Message Format Variation (Intentional)\n408\t\n409\t**Issue:** Error messages vary in verbosity\n410\t- Auth errors use generic messages (e.g., \"Access Denied\")\n411\t- Internal errors include diagnostic details (e.g., \"Failed to encrypt: {error}\")\n412\t\n413\t**Impact:** None - this is security best practice\n414\t\n415\t**Current Status:** ✅ Working as designed\n416\t\n417\t**Recommendation:** No change needed - maintain this pattern for security\n418\t\n419\t#### 4. Admin Interface Response Format Inconsistencies (Medium Severity)\n420\t\n421\t**Issue:** Administrative and management endpoints use mixed response formats\n422\t\n423\t**Current Behavior:**\n424\t- `/admin/b2/keys/*` endpoints return plain text errors with `Content-Type: text/plain`\n425\t- `/admin/key/*` endpoints return plain text errors with `Content-Type: text/plain`\n426\t- `/presign` endpoint returns plain text errors with `Content-Type: text/plain`\n427\t- `/l/{token}` public link endpoints return plain text errors\n428\t\n429\t**Impact:**\n430\t- Inconsistent API experience for consumers\n431\t- S3-facing endpoints (`/presign`) should return XML for S3 compatibility\n432\t- Admin endpoints lack standardized error format\n433\t\n434\t**Recommendation:**\n435\t- **Priority 1 (High):** Convert `/presign` endpoint to return XML errors (S3-facing)\n436\t- **Priority 2 (Medium):** Standardize admin endpoints on JSON format:\n437\t  ```json\n438\t  {\n439\t    \"error\": \"ErrorCode\",\n440\t    \"message\": \"Detailed error message\"\n441\t  }\n442\t  ```\n443\t- **Priority 3 (Low):** Public link endpoints may continue using plain text (non-S3)\n444\t\n445\t**Tracking:** Documented in bead bf-649uw6 header consistency verification\n446\t\n447\t#### 5. Method Not Allowed Errors Return Plain Text (Medium Severity)\n448\t\n449\t**Issue:** HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed errors return plain text instead of XML\n450\t\n451\t**Current Behavior:**\n452\t```http\n453\tHTTP/1.1 405 Method Not Allowed\n454\tContent-Type: text/plain\n455\t\n456\tMethod not allowed\n457\t```\n458\t\n459\t**Expected Behavior (for S3 endpoints):**\n460\t```http\n461\tHTTP/1.1 405 Method Not Allowed\n462\tContent-Type: application/xml\n463\t\n464\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n465\t<Error>\n466\t  <Code>MethodNotAllowed</Code>\n467\t  <Message>Method PUT is not allowed for this resource</Message>\n468\t</Error>\n469\t```\n470\t\n471\t**Impact:**\n472\t- S3 clients expecting XML may fail to parse 405 errors\n473\t- Inconsistency with S3 protocol specification\n474\t\n475\t**Affected Endpoints:**\n476\t- `/admin/key/verify`\n477\t- `/admin/key/rotate`\n478\t- `/admin/key/export`\n479\t- `/presign`\n480\t- `/admin/b2/keys` (all methods)\n481\t\n482\t**Recommendation:**\n483\t- Convert all S3-facing endpoints to return XML for 405 errors\n484\t- Internal admin endpoints may use JSON or plain text\n485\t\n486\t**Tracking:** Documented in bead bf-649uw6 header consistency verification\n487\t\n488\t### Coverage Gaps\n489\t\n490\tThe following error scenarios are **covered by code but lack explicit test cases**:\n491\t\n492\t| Error Code | Coverage Status | Note |\n493\t|------------|-----------------|------|\n494\t| `InvalidCredential` | Code path verified | Flows through same `writeError` with 403 |\n495\t| `InvalidDateFormat` | Code path verified | Flows through same `writeError` with 403 |\n496\t| `AccessDenied` | Code path verified | Flows through same `writeError` with 403 |\n497\t\n498\t**Impact:** Low - all scenarios use verified code paths\n499\t\n500\t**Recommendation:** Optional - consider adding explicit test cases for completeness\n501\t\n502\t### Remediation Plan for Inconsistencies\n503\t\n504\t#### Priority 1 (S3 Protocol Compliance)\n505\t\n506\t| Issue | Effort | Target Release | Action |\n507\t|-------|--------|----------------|--------|\n508\t| Method Not Allowed XML format | Low | Q3 2026 | Convert 405 errors to XML for S3 endpoints |\n509\t| Presigned URL XML format | Low | Q3 2026 | Convert `/presign` errors to XML |\n510\t\n511\t#### Priority 2 (Admin Interface Consistency)\n512\t\n513\t| Issue | Effort | Target Release | Action |\n514\t|-------|--------|----------------|--------|\n515\t| Admin API JSON format | Medium | Q4 2026 | Standardize admin endpoints on JSON |\n516\t\n517\t#### Implementation Notes\n518\t\n519\t**For Method Not Allowed (405) errors:**\n520\t- Update all `http.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", 405)` calls\n521\t- Use centralized XML error response function instead\n522\t- Ensure proper S3 `MethodNotAllowed` error code\n523\t\n524\t**For Presigned URL endpoint:**\n525\t- Convert input validation errors to XML format\n526\t- Maintain error messages while changing format\n527\t- Update tests to verify XML structure\n528\t\n529\t**For Admin endpoints:**\n530\t- Design JSON error schema\n531\t- Implement centralized JSON error response function\n532\t- Update all admin endpoint error paths\n533\t- Add JSON error response tests\n534\t\n535\t## Performance Requirements\n536\t\n537\t### Response Time Requirements\n538\t\n539\tAll error responses must meet these performance targets:\n540\t\n541\t| Test Type | Target | Actual | Environment |\n542\t|-----------|--------|--------|-------------|\n543\t| Unit test rejections | < 100ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n544\t| Integration test rejections | < 500ms | < 50ms | Real server |\n545\t| Malformed signature rejections | < 50ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n546\t\n547\t**Average Response Time:** < 150µs (measured 2026-07-14)\n548\t\n549\t### Header Setting Performance\n550\t\n551\tHeader setting must not significantly impact response time:\n552\t\n553\t- Single `WriteHeader()` call per response\n554\t- No blocking operations during header generation\n555\t- Header order must not affect performance\n556\t\n557\t## Testing Requirements\n558\t\n559\t### Required Test Coverage\n560\t\n561\tFor any new error response or modification to existing error responses, the following tests are required:\n562\t\n563\t1. **Header verification test** - Verify all required headers are present\n564\t2. **Content-Type test** - Verify `application/xml` header\n565\t3. **Status code test** - Verify correct HTTP status code\n566\t4. **XML structure test** - Verify XML format and escaping\n567\t5. **Performance test** - Verify response time meets targets\n568\t\n569\t### Existing Test Suites\n570\t\n571\tThe following test files verify header consistency:\n572\t\n573\t- `internal/server/invalid_credential_test.go` - Auth rejection scenarios\n574\t- `internal/server/malformed_signature_test.go` - Malformed signature scenarios\n575\t- `internal/server/invalid_credential_integration_test.go` - Integration tests\n576\t- `internal/server/content_type_consistency_test.go` - Content-Type verification\n577\t- `internal/server/error_response_verification_test.go` - Comprehensive error verification\n578\t\n579\t### Running Tests\n580\t\n581\t```bash\n582\t# Run all error response tests\n583\tgo test -v -run \"TestInvalidCredentialRejection|TestMalformedSignatureRejection\" ./internal/server/\n584\t\n585\t# Run Content-Type consistency test\n586\tgo test -v -run TestContentTypeConsistencyAcrossAllRejections ./internal/server/\n587\t\n588\t# Run comprehensive error verification\n589\tgo test -v -run TestComprehensiveErrorVerification ./internal/server/\n590\t\n591\t# Run integration tests (requires INTEGRATION_TEST=1)\n592\tINTEGRATION_TEST=1 go test -v -run TestInvalidCredentialsIntegration ./internal/server/\n593\t```\n594\t\n595\t## S3 Compliance\n596\t\n597\tThis specification ensures AWS S3 API compatibility:\n598\t\n599\t### S3 Error Response Compliance\n600\t\n601\t✅ **Compliant** with [AWS S3 Error Responses](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/ErrorResponses.html):\n602\t\n603\t- XML format matches S3 specification\n604\t- Error codes match S3 error codes\n605\t- HTTP status codes match S3 behavior\n606\t- Content-Type header matches S3 (`application/xml`)\n607\t\n608\t### Deviations from S3\n609\t\n610\tNone detected - ARMOR error responses are fully S3-compatible\n611\t\n612\t## Implementation Checklist\n613\t\n614\tWhen implementing a new error response or modifying existing error responses, use this checklist:\n615\t\n616\t- [ ] Set `Content-Type: application/xml` header\n617\t- [ ] Use appropriate HTTP status code (see HTTP Status Codes section)\n618\t- [ ] Use S3-compatible error code (PascalCase)\n619\t- [ ] Provide meaningful error message\n620\t- [ ] Follow XML structure specification\n621\t- [ ] Escape XML special characters\n622\t- [ ] Add test coverage for new error\n623\t- [ ] Verify response time < 100ms\n624\t- [ ] Update this specification if new error type added\n625\t- [ ] For 403 errors: ensure CORS headers present\n626\t\n627\t## Maintenance\n628\t\n629\t### Version History\n630\t\n631\t| Version | Date | HTTP status code\n704\t3. XML declaration\n705\t4. `<Error>` root element\n706\t5. `<Code>` element\n707\t6. `<Message>` element\n708\t\n709\t---\n710\t\n711\t**End of Specification**\n712\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_4ba177b7bb03455d8bf4870a","session_id":"b39f11cf-5041-46b3-9bc8-23d307471316","uuid":"75ed166f-76ae-4d40-8351-709dd5f02ebc","timestamp":"2026-07-14T05:39:56.008Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Find S3 endpoint implementation"}

document documents the exact response headers returned for all authentication rejection scenarios.\n4\t\n5\t## Overview\n6\t\n7\tAll authentication rejection scenarios return consistent response headers with an HTTP status code of 403 Forbidden and an XML-formatted error response body.\n8\t\n9\t## Common Response Headers (All Scenarios)\n10\t\n11\tAll authentication rejection scenarios return the following headers:\n12\t\n13\t```\n14\tContent-Type: application/xml\n15\tAccess-Control-Allow-Origin: *\n16\tAccess-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\n17\tAccess-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\n18\t```\n19\t\n20\t**Note:** Header order may vary between responses, but the same set of headers is always present.\n21\t\n22\t## Authentication Rejection Scenarios\n23\t\n24\t### MissingAuthenticationToken\n25\t\n26\t**Trigger:** Authorization header is missing\n27\t\n28\t**HTTP Status Code:** `403`\n29\t\n30\t**Response Headers:**\n31\t```\n32\tContent-Type: application/xml\n33\tAccess-Control-Allow-Origin: *\n34\tAccess-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\n35\tAccess-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\n36\t```\n37\t\n38\t**Response Body:**\n39\t```xml\n40\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n41\t<Error>\n42\t  <Code>MissingAuthenticationToken</Code>\n43\t  <Message>Missing Authentication Token</Message>\n44\t</Error>\n45\t```\n46\t\n47\t**Error Details:**\n48\t- **Error Code:** `MissingAuthenticationToken`\n49\t- **Error Message:** Missing Authentication Token\n50\t\n51\t---\n52\t\n53\t### InvalidAccessKeyId\n54\t\n55\t**Trigger:** The provided access key does not exist\n56\t\n57\t**HTTP Status Code:** `403`\n58\t\n59\t**Response Headers:**\n60\t```\n61\tAccess-Control-Allow-Origin: *\n62\tAccess-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\n63\tAccess-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\n64\tContent-Type: application/xml\n65\t```\n66\t\n67\t**Response Body:**\n68\t```xml\n69\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n70\t<Error>\n71\t  <Code>InvalidAccessKeyId</Code>\n72\t  <Message>The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist</Message>\n73\t</Error>\n74\t```\n75\t\n76\t**Error Details:**\n77\t- **Error Code:** `InvalidAccessKeyId`\n78\t- **Error Message:** The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist\n79\t\n80\t---\n81\t\n82\t### SignatureDoesNotMatch\n83\t\n84\t**Trigger:** Calculated signature does not match provided signature\n85\t\n86\t**HTTP Status Code:** `403`\n87\t\n88\t**Response Headers:**\n89\t```\n90\tAccess-Control-Allow-Origin: *\n91\tAccess-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\n92\tAccess-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\n93\tContent-Type: application/xml\n94\t```\n95\t\n96\t**Response Body:**\n97\t```xml\n98\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n99\t<Error>\n100\t `IncompleteSignature`\n223\t- **Error Message:** Authorization header is missing required fields\n224\t\n225\t---\n226\t\n227\t## Consistency Summary\n228\t\n229\t### Header Consistency\n230\t✅ **All Authentication/Authorization Errors (403 Forbidden)\n56\t\n57\t| Error Code | Trigger | Message |\n58\t|------------|---------|---------|\n59\t| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | Authorization header is missing | \"Missing Authentication Token\" |\n60\t| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | Invalid access key provided | \"The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist\" |\n61\t| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | Calculated signature doesn't match | \"The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided\" |\n62\t| `RequestExpired` | Request timestamp outside 15-minute window | \"Request has expired\" |\n63\t| `InvalidAlgorithm` | Non-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 algorithm | \"Only AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 is supported\" |\n64\t| `IncompleteSignature` | Authorization header missing fields | \"Authorization header is missing required fields\" |\n65\t| `MissingDateHeader` | X-Amz-Date header missing | \"Missing X-Amz-Date header\" |\n66\t| `InvalidDateFormat` | X-Amz-Date format invalid | \"Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header\" |\n67\t| `AccessDenied` | ACL-based access control rejection | \"Access Denied\" |\n68\t\n69\t**Headers:** `Content-Type: application/xml`, `HTTP/1.1 403`\n70\t\n71\t### 2. Client Input Errors (400 Bad Request / 405 Method Not Allowed)\n72\t\n73\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Trigger | Message |\n74\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------|\n75\t| `InvalidRequest` | 400 | Unsupported POST operation, missing/invalid parameters | Varies |\n76\t| `MethodNotAllowed` | 405 | HTTP method not supported | \"Method {method} not allowed\" |\n77\t| `InvalidRange` | 400 | Invalid Range header format | \"Invalid range: {details}\" |\n78\t| `MalformedXML` | 400 | Failed to parse request XML | \"Failed to parse XML: {details}\" |\n79\t\n80\t**Headers:** `Content-Type: application/xml`, `HTTP/1.1 400` or `HTTP/1.1 405`\n81\t\n82\t### 3. Resource Not Found Errors (404 Not Found)\n83\t\n84\t| Error Code | Trigger | Message |\n85\t|------------|---------|---------|\n86\t| `NoSuchKey` | Object does not exist | \"Object not found\" |\n87\t| `NoSuchBucket` | Bucket does not exist | \"Bucket not found\" |\n88\t| `NoSuchUpload` | Multipart upload ID not found | \"Multipart upload not found\" |\n89\t\n90\t**Headers:** `Content-Type: application/xml`, `HTTP/1.1 404`\n91\t\n92\t### 4. Conditional Request Errors (412 Precondition Failed)\n93\t\n94\t| Error Code | Trigger | Message |\n95\t|------------|---------|---------|\n96\t| `PreconditionFailed` | If-Match/If-Unmodified-Since condition failed | \"Precondition failed\" |\n97\t\n98\t**Headers:** `Content-Type: application/xml`, `HTTP/1.1 412`\n99\t\n100\t### 5. Internal Server Errors (500 Internal Server Error)\n101\t\n102\t| Error Code | Trigger | Message |\n103\t|------------|---------|---------|\n104\t| `InternalError` | Backend failures, encryption/decryption errors | Varies: \"Failed to {operation}: {error}\" |\n105\t\n106\t**Headers:** `Content-Type: application/xml`, `HTTP/1.1 500`\n107\t\n108\t### 6. Service Unavailable (503 Service Unavailable)\n109\t\n110\t| Error Code | Trigger | Message |\n111\t|------------|---------|---------|\n112\t| `ServiceUnavailable` | Health check failures | Varies |\n113\t\n114\t**Headers:** `Content-Type: application/xml`, `HTTP/1.1 503`\n115\t\n116\t## Consistency Verification\n117\t\n118\t### ✅ Consistent Headers\n119\t\n120\tAll error responses across **all rejection types** consistently set:\n121\t- **`Content-Type: application/xml`** - Always set, no exceptions\n122\t- **HTTP status code** - Appropriate for error category\n123\t\n124\t### ✅ Consistent Response Format\n125\t\n126\tAll error responses follow the same XML structure:\n127\t- XML declaration: `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`\n128\t- Root element: `<Error>`\n129\t- Code element: `<Code>`\n130\t- Message element: `<Message>`\n131\t\n132\t### ✅ XML Escaping\n133\t\n134\tBoth `writeError` implementations properly escape XML special characters in code and message fields, preventing injection attacks.\n135\t\n136\t## Potential Minor Inconsistencies\n137\t\n138\t### 1. Message Format Variation\n139\t\n140\t**Status:** ⚠️ Minor inconsistency in message verbosity\n141\t\n142\tSome error messages include detailed error context (e.g., `\"Failed to encrypt: {error}\"`), while others are static strings (e.g., `\"Access Denied\"`). Error Code Casing\n147\t\n148\t**Status:** ✅ Consistent\n149\t\n150\tAll error codes use PascalCase (e.g., `SignatureDoesNotMatch`, `InternalError`), matching AWS S3 conventions.\n151\t\n152\t### 3. Duplicate writeError Implementations\n153\t\n154\t**Status:** ⚠️ Code duplication\n155\t\n156\tBoth `server.go` and `handlers.go` contain identical `writeError` functions. This creates maintenance risk if one is updated without the other.\n157\t\n158\t**Recommendation:** Consider extracting to a shared utility function.\n159\t\n160\t## Header Consistency Summary Table\n161\t\n162\t| Error Category | HTTP Status | Content-Type | Other Headers |\n163\t|----------------|-------------|--------------|---------------|\n164\t| Authentication failures | 403 | application/xml | None |\n165\t| Authorization failures | 403 | application/xml | None |\n166\t| Input validation errors | 400 | application/xml | None |\n167\t| Method not allowed | 405 | application/xml | None |\n168\t| Resource not found | 404 | application/xml | None |\n169\t| Precondition failed | 412 | application/xml | None |\n170\t| Internal errors | 500 | application/xml | None |\n171\t| Service unavailable | 503 | application/xml | None |\n172\t\n173\t## Verification Results\n174\t\n175\t### Acceptance Criteria Status\n176\t\n177\t| Criterion | Status | Notes |\n178\t|-----------|--------|-------|\n179\t| Headers documented for each rejection scenario | ✅ Complete | All error types documented with headers |\n180\t| Inconsistent headers identified and documented | ✅ Complete | No functional inconsistencies found; {"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_202607141341158eceba95ff634cf2","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_25bcad5391444c37b6c90d63","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/coding/ARMOR/docs/s3-endpoint-response-headers.md","content":"# ARMOR S3 Endpoint Response Headers\n\n**Version:** 1.0  \n**Date:** 2026-07-14  \n**Status:** Active\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document provides a focused inventory of all response headers returned by ARMOR's S3-facing endpoints, including both success and error responses. It serves as a quick reference for understanding ARMOR's S3 API compatibility.\n\n**Related Documentation:**\n- [Error Response Header Specification](./error-header-spec.md) - Comprehensive error response documentation\n- [Error Responses](./error-responses.md) - Detailed error response documentation\n\n## Response Header Categories\n\n### 1. Standard HTTP Headers (All Responses)\n\n| Header | Presence | Description |\n|--------|----------|-------------|\n| `Content-Type` | All responses | `application/xml` for errors; varies for success (object content type) |\n| `Content-Length` | Success responses | Size of response body (not set for error responses) |\n| `Content-Range` | Partial GET | Range response format: `bytes {start}-{end}/{total}` |\n| `Content-Disposition` | Conditional | Set when object has `Content-Disposition` metadata |\n| `Accept-Ranges` | HEAD/GET | Always `bytes` (indicates range request support) |\n\n### 2. S3-Specific Headers (Success Responses)\n\n| Header | Format | When Returned | Example |\n|--------|--------|---------------|---------|\n| `ETag` | `\"{md5hash}\"` | PUT success, GET/HEAD success, ListObjects entries | `\"5d41402abc4b2a76b9719d911017c592\"` |\n| `Last-Modified` | HTTP TimeFormat | GET/HEAD object, ListObjects entries | `Mon, 14 Jul 2026 05:00:00 GMT` |\n\n### 3. CORS Headers (HTTP 403 Only)\n\n| Header | Value | When Returned |\n|--------|-------|---------------|\n| `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` | `*` | HTTP 403 authentication/authorization errors only |\n| `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` | `GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS` | HTTP 403 authentication/authorization errors only |\n| `Access-Control-Allow-Headers` | `Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length` | HTTP 403 authentication/authorization errors only |\n\n### 4. ARMOR-Specific Headers\n\n| Header | Value | When Returned |\n|--------|-------|---------------|\n| `X-Armor-Streaming` | `true` | PUT object with streaming encryption (>10MB objects) |\n\n## Error Response Headers by HTTP Status\n\n### HTTP 400 Bad Request\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `InvalidRequest` - Unsupported operations, invalid parameters\n- `InvalidRange` - Invalid Range header format\n- `MalformedXML` - Failed to parse request XML\n\n**No CORS headers**\n\n### HTTP 403 Forbidden\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\nAccess-Control-Allow-Origin: *\nAccess-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\nAccess-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `MissingAuthenticationToken` - Authorization header missing\n- `InvalidAccessKeyId` - Invalid access key\n- `SignatureDoesNotMatch` - Signature validation failed\n- `RequestExpired` - Request timestamp outside 15-minute window\n- `InvalidAlgorithm` - Non-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 algorithm\n- `IncompleteSignature` - Authorization header missing fields\n- `MissingDateHeader` - X-Amz-Date header missing\n- `InvalidDateFormat` - Invalid date format\n- `InvalidCredential` - Invalid credential format\n- `AccessDenied` - ACL-based authorization rejection\n\n**CORS headers present** (only error type with CORS)\n\n### HTTP 404 Not Found\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `NoSuchKey` - Object not found\n- `NoSuchBucket` - Bucket not found\n- `NoSuchUpload` - Multipart upload not found\n\n**No CORS headers**\n\n### HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `MethodNotAllowed` - HTTP method not supported\n\n**No CORS headers**\n\n### HTTP 412 Precondition Failed\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `PreconditionFailed` - If-Match/If-Unmodified-Since condition failed\n\n**No CORS headers**\n\n### HTTP 500 Internal Server Error\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `InternalError` - Backend failures, encryption/decryption errors\n\n**No CORS headers**\n\n### HTTP 503 Service Unavailable\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `ServiceUnavailable` - Health check failures\n\n**No CORS headers**\n\n## Success Response Headers by Operation\n\n### GET Object (200)\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: {object-content-type}\nContent-Length: {object-size}\nETag: \"{etag-md5}\"\nLast-Modified: {http-timeformat}\nAccept-Ranges: bytes\nContent-Disposition: {disposition}  (if present in metadata)\nContent-Range: bytes {start}-{end}/{total}  (for Range requests)\n```\n\n**Conditional Request Responses (304):**\n- Returns `304 Not Modified` with no body when `If-Match`/`If-None-Match`/`If-Modified-Since`/`If-Unmodified-Since` conditions are met\n\n### HEAD Object (200)\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: {object-content-type}\nContent-Length: {object-size}\nETag: \"{etag-md5}\"\nLast-Modified: {http-timeformat}\nAccept-Ranges: bytes\n```\n\n**No response body**\n\n### PUT Object (200)\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nETag: \"{etag-md5}\"\nX-Armor-Streaming: true  (for >10MB objects using streaming encryption)\n```\n\n**No response body**\n\n### DELETE Object (204)\n\n**Headers:**\n```\n(no headers)\n```\n\n**No response body** (HTTP 204 No Content)\n\n### ListObjectsV2 (200)\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Response Body:** XML with `Contents` entries including `ETag` and `Last-Modified` for each object\n\n### CopyObject (200)\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Response Body:** XML with destination object's `ETag` and `Last-Modified`\n\n## Deviations from AWS S3\n\n### Missing Standard S3 Response Headers\n\nARMOR does **not** return the following standard AWS S3 response headers:\n\n| Missing Header | AWS Purpose | Impact |\n|----------------|-------------|--------|\n| `x-amz-request-id` | AWS request tracking ID | Medium - prevents request tracing in AWS-compatible tools |\n| `x-amz-id-2` | AWS extended request ID (S3-specific) | Low - rarely used by clients |\n\n**Recommendation:** Consider adding `x-amz-request-id` header with a UUID for each request to improve AWS compatibility and request tracing.\n\n### Non-Standard Behaviors\n\n1. **ETag for Streaming Encryption:**\n   - **AWS S3:** ETag is always MD5 hash of object content (multi-part objects have different ETag format)\n   - **ARMOR:** For >10MB objects using streaming encryption, ETag is SHA-256 truncated to 16 bytes\n   - **Impact:** Low - ETag format is opaque to most clients, but may affect conditional requests\n   - **Mitigation:** ARMOR sets `X-Armor-Streaming: true` header to indicate this case\n\n2. **405 Method Not Allowed (Non-S3 Endpoints):**\n   - **AWS S3:** Would return XML error response\n   - **ARMOR:** Admin endpoints (e.g., `/admin/key/*`) return plain text \"Method not allowed\"\n   - **Impact:** Low - admin endpoints are not S3-facing\n   - **Status:** Documented in error-header-spec.md as Priority 2 remediation\n\n## Quick Reference Tables\n\n### Error Response Summary\n\n| HTTP Status | Content-Type | CORS | Error Codes |\n|-------------|--------------|------|-------------|\n| 400 | application/xml | No | InvalidRequest, InvalidRange, MalformedXML |\n| 403 | application/xml | Yes | 10 auth error codes (MissingAuthenticationToken, etc.) |\n| 404 | application/xml | No | NoSuchKey, NoSuchBucket, NoSuchUpload |\n| 405 | application/xml | No | MethodNotAllowed |\n| 412 | application/xml | No | PreconditionFailed |\n| 500 | application/xml | No | InternalError |\n| 503 | application/xml | No | ServiceUnavailable |\n\n### Success Response Header Summary\n\n| Operation | ETag | Last-Modified | Accept-Ranges | Other |\n|-----------|------|---------------|----------------|-------|\n| GET Object | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Content-Type, Content-Length, Content-Range (if Range) |\n| HEAD Object | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Content-Type, Content-Length |\n| PUT Object | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | X-Armor-Streaming (if >10MB) |\n| DELETE Object | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | (no headers, 204 response) |\n| ListObjectsV2 | ✓ (in body) | ✓ (in body) | ✗ | Content-Type: application/xml |\n\n## Implementation Files\n\n- **Error Responses:** `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:2695-2704` (`writeError`)\n- **Success Responses:** \n  - GET Object: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:602` (`GetObject`)\n  - HEAD Object: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:1133` (`HeadObject`)\n  - PUT Object: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:267` (`PutObject`)\n  - DELETE Object: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:1210` (`DeleteObject`)\n  - ListObjectsV2: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:1470`\n  - CopyObject: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:1238`\n  - CORS: `internal/server/server.go:656-660`\n\n## Testing\n\nSee [Error Response Header Specification](./error-header-spec.md#testing-requirements) for comprehensive testing documentation.\n\n## References\n\n### AWS S3 Documentation\n- [S3 Response Headers](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTCommonResponseHeaders.html)\n- [S3 Error Responses](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/ErrorResponses.html)\n\n### Internal Documentation\n- [Error Response Header Specification](./error-header-spec.md)\n- [Error Responses](./error-responses.md)\n- [Authentication Rejection Headers](./auth-rejection-headers.md)\n\n---\n\n**End of Document**\n"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b39f11cf-5041-46b3-9bc8-23d307471316","uuid":"bd496111-8ae0-4d7f-83ab-34325bd13a1a"}
